<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].podAffinityTerm.namespaceSelector.matchExpressions[*].key">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].podAffinityTerm.namespaceSelector.matchExpressions[*].key</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>key is the label key that the selector applies to.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-12">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].podAffinityTerm.namespaceSelector.matchExpressions[*].operator">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].podAffinityTerm.namespaceSelector.matchExpressions[*].operator</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>operator represents a key&rsquo;s relationship to a set of values.
Valid operators are In, NotIn, Exists and DoesNotExist.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-12">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].podAffinityTerm.namespaceSelector.matchExpressions[*].values">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].podAffinityTerm.namespaceSelector.matchExpressions[*].values</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>values is an array of string values. If the operator is In or NotIn,
the values array must be non-empty. If the operator is Exists or DoesNotExist,
the values array must be empty. This array is replaced during a strategic
merge patch.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-13">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].podAffinityTerm.namespaceSelector.matchExpressions[*].values[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].podAffinityTerm.namespaceSelector.matchExpressions[*].values[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].podAffinityTerm.namespaceSelector.matchLabels">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].podAffinityTerm.namespaceSelector.matchLabels</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>matchLabels is a map of {key,value} pairs. A single {key,value} in the matchLabels
map is equivalent to an element of matchExpressions, whose key field is &ldquo;key&rdquo;, the
operator is &ldquo;In&rdquo;, and the values array contains only &ldquo;value&rdquo;. The requirements are ANDed.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].podAffinityTerm.namespaces">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].podAffinityTerm.namespaces</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>namespaces specifies a static list of namespace names that the term applies to.
The term is applied to the union of the namespaces listed in this field
and the ones selected by namespaceSelector.
null or empty namespaces list and null namespaceSelector means &ldquo;this pod&rsquo;s namespace&rdquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].podAffinityTerm.namespaces[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].podAffinityTerm.namespaces[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].podAffinityTerm.topologyKey">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].podAffinityTerm.topologyKey</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>This pod should be co-located (affinity) or not co-located (anti-affinity) with the pods matching
the labelSelector in the specified namespaces, where co-located is defined as running on a node
whose value of the label with key topologyKey matches that of any node on which any of the
selected pods is running.
Empty topologyKey is not allowed.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].weight">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].weight</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>weight associated with matching the corresponding podAffinityTerm,
in the range 1-100.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>If the anti-affinity requirements specified by this field are not met at
scheduling time, the pod will not be scheduled onto the node.
If the anti-affinity requirements specified by this field cease to be met
at some point during pod execution (e.g. due to a pod label update), the
system may or may not try to eventually evict the pod from its node.
When there are multiple elements, the lists of nodes corresponding to each
podAffinityTerm are intersected, i.e. all terms must be satisfied.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Defines a set of pods (namely those matching the labelSelector
relative to the given namespace(s)) that this pod should be
co-located (affinity) or not co-located (anti-affinity) with,
where co-located is defined as running on a node whose value of
the label with key <topologyKey> matches that of any node on which
a pod of the set of pods is running</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].labelSelector">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].labelSelector</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>A label query over a set of resources, in this case pods.
If it&rsquo;s null, this PodAffinityTerm matches with no Pods.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].labelSelector.matchExpressions">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].labelSelector.matchExpressions</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>matchExpressions is a list of label selector requirements. The requirements are ANDed.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].labelSelector.matchExpressions[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].labelSelector.matchExpressions[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>A label selector requirement is a selector that contains values, a key, and an operator that
relates the key and values.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-11">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].labelSelector.matchExpressions[*].key">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].labelSelector.matchExpressions[*].key</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>key is the label key that the selector applies to.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-11">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].labelSelector.matchExpressions[*].operator">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].labelSelector.matchExpressions[*].operator</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>operator represents a key&rsquo;s relationship to a set of values.
Valid operators are In, NotIn, Exists and DoesNotExist.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-11">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].labelSelector.matchExpressions[*].values">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].labelSelector.matchExpressions[*].values</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>values is an array of string values. If the operator is In or NotIn,
the values array must be non-empty. If the operator is Exists or DoesNotExist,
the values array must be empty. This array is replaced during a strategic
merge patch.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-12">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].labelSelector.matchExpressions[*].values[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].labelSelector.matchExpressions[*].values[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].labelSelector.matchLabels">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].labelSelector.matchLabels</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>matchLabels is a map of {key,value} pairs. A single {key,value} in the matchLabels
map is equivalent to an element of matchExpressions, whose key field is &ldquo;key&rdquo;, the
operator is &ldquo;In&rdquo;, and the values array contains only &ldquo;value&rdquo;. The requirements are ANDed.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].matchLabelKeys">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].matchLabelKeys</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>MatchLabelKeys is a set of pod label keys to select which pods will
be taken into consideration. The keys are used to lookup values from the
incoming pod labels, those key-value labels are merged with <code>labelSelector</code> as <code>key in (value)</code>
to select the group of existing pods which pods will be taken into consideration
for the incoming pod&rsquo;s pod (anti) affinity. Keys that don&rsquo;t exist in the incoming
pod labels will be ignored. The default value is empty.
The same key is forbidden to exist in both matchLabelKeys and labelSelector.
Also, matchLabelKeys cannot be set when labelSelector isn&rsquo;t set.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].matchLabelKeys[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].matchLabelKeys[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].mismatchLabelKeys">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].mismatchLabelKeys</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>MismatchLabelKeys is a set of pod label keys to select which pods will
be taken into consideration. The keys are used to lookup values from the
incoming pod labels, those key-value labels are merged with <code>labelSelector</code> as <code>key notin (value)</code>
to select the group of existing pods which pods will be taken into consideration
for the incoming pod&rsquo;s pod (anti) affinity. Keys that don&rsquo;t exist in the incoming
pod labels will be ignored. The default value is empty.
The same key is forbidden to exist in both mismatchLabelKeys and labelSelector.
Also, mismatchLabelKeys cannot be set when labelSelector isn&rsquo;t set.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].mismatchLabelKeys[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].mismatchLabelKeys[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaceSelector">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaceSelector</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>A label query over the set of namespaces that the term applies to.
The term is applied to the union of the namespaces selected by this field
and the ones listed in the namespaces field.
null selector and null or empty namespaces list means &ldquo;this pod&rsquo;s namespace&rdquo;.
An empty selector ({}) matches all namespaces.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaceSelector.matchExpressions">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaceSelector.matchExpressions</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>matchExpressions is a list of label selector requirements. The requirements are ANDed.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaceSelector.matchExpressions[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaceSelector.matchExpressions[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>A label selector requirement is a selector that contains values, a key, and an operator that
relates the key and values.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-11">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaceSelector.matchExpressions[*].key">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaceSelector.matchExpressions[*].key</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>key is the label key that the selector applies to.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-11">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaceSelector.matchExpressions[*].operator">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaceSelector.matchExpressions[*].operator</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>operator represents a key&rsquo;s relationship to a set of values.
Valid operators are In, NotIn, Exists and DoesNotExist.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-11">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaceSelector.matchExpressions[*].values">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaceSelector.matchExpressions[*].values</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>values is an array of string values. If the operator is In or NotIn,
the values array must be non-empty. If the operator is Exists or DoesNotExist,
the values array must be empty. This array is replaced during a strategic
merge patch.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-12">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaceSelector.matchExpressions[*].values[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaceSelector.matchExpressions[*].values[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaceSelector.matchLabels">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaceSelector.matchLabels</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>matchLabels is a map of {key,value} pairs. A single {key,value} in the matchLabels
map is equivalent to an element of matchExpressions, whose key field is &ldquo;key&rdquo;, the
operator is &ldquo;In&rdquo;, and the values array contains only &ldquo;value&rdquo;. The requirements are ANDed.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaces">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaces</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>namespaces specifies a static list of namespace names that the term applies to.
The term is applied to the union of the namespaces listed in this field
and the ones selected by namespaceSelector.
null or empty namespaces list and null namespaceSelector means &ldquo;this pod&rsquo;s namespace&rdquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaces[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaces[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].topologyKey">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].topologyKey</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>This pod should be co-located (affinity) or not co-located (anti-affinity) with the pods matching
the labelSelector in the specified namespaces, where co-located is defined as running on a node
whose value of the label with key topologyKey matches that of any node on which any of the
selected pods is running.
Empty topologyKey is not allowed.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.brokerAnnotations">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.brokerAnnotations</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Custom annotations for the broker pods - e.g.: Prometheus scraping annotations:
prometheus.io/scrape: &ldquo;true&rdquo;
prometheus.io/port: &ldquo;9020&rdquo;</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.brokerIngressMapping">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.brokerIngressMapping</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>BrokerIngressMapping allows to set specific ingress to a specific broker mappings.
If left empty, all broker will inherit the default one specified under external listeners config
Only used when ExternalListeners.Config is populated</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.brokerIngressMapping[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.brokerIngressMapping[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.brokerLabels">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.brokerLabels</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Custom labels for the broker pods, example use case: for Prometheus monitoring to capture the group for each broker as a label, e.g.:
kafka_broker_group: &ldquo;default_group&rdquo;
these labels will not override the reserved labels that the operator relies on, for example, &ldquo;app&rdquo;, &ldquo;brokerId&rdquo;, and &ldquo;kafka_cr&rdquo;</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.config">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.config</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Containers add extra Containers to the Kafka broker pod</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>A single application container that you want to run within a pod.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].args">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].args</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Arguments to the entrypoint.
The container image&rsquo;s CMD is used if this is not provided.
Variable references $(VAR_NAME) are expanded using the container&rsquo;s environment. If a variable
cannot be resolved, the reference in the input string will be unchanged. Double $$ are reduced
to a single $, which allows for escaping the $(VAR_NAME) syntax: i.e. &ldquo;$$(VAR_NAME)&rdquo; will
produce the string literal &ldquo;$(VAR_NAME)&rdquo;. Escaped references will never be expanded, regardless
of whether the variable exists or not. Cannot be updated.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-command-argument-container/#running-a-command-in-a-shell">https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-command-argument-container/#running-a-command-in-a-shell</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].args[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].args[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].command">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].command</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Entrypoint array. Not executed within a shell.
The container image&rsquo;s ENTRYPOINT is used if this is not provided.
Variable references $(VAR_NAME) are expanded using the container&rsquo;s environment. If a variable
cannot be resolved, the reference in the input string will be unchanged. Double $$ are reduced
to a single $, which allows for escaping the $(VAR_NAME) syntax: i.e. &ldquo;$$(VAR_NAME)&rdquo; will
produce the string literal &ldquo;$(VAR_NAME)&rdquo;. Escaped references will never be expanded, regardless
of whether the variable exists or not. Cannot be updated.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-command-argument-container/#running-a-command-in-a-shell">https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-command-argument-container/#running-a-command-in-a-shell</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].command[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].command[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>List of environment variables to set in the container.
Cannot be updated.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].envFrom">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].envFrom</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>List of sources to populate environment variables in the container.
The keys defined within a source may consist of any printable ASCII characters except &lsquo;=&rsquo;.
When a key exists in multiple
sources, the value associated with the last source will take precedence.
Values defined by an Env with a duplicate key will take precedence.
Cannot be updated.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].envFrom[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].envFrom[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>EnvFromSource represents the source of a set of ConfigMaps or Secrets</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].envFrom[*].configMapRef">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].envFrom[*].configMapRef</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The ConfigMap to select from</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].envFrom[*].configMapRef.name">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].envFrom[*].configMapRef.name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].envFrom[*].configMapRef.optional">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].envFrom[*].configMapRef.optional</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Specify whether the ConfigMap must be defined</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].envFrom[*].prefix">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].envFrom[*].prefix</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Optional text to prepend to the name of each environment variable.
May consist of any printable ASCII characters except &lsquo;=&rsquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].envFrom[*].secretRef">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].envFrom[*].secretRef</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The Secret to select from</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].envFrom[*].secretRef.name">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].envFrom[*].secretRef.name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].envFrom[*].secretRef.optional">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].envFrom[*].secretRef.optional</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Specify whether the Secret must be defined</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>EnvVar represents an environment variable present in a Container.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].name">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name of the environment variable.
May consist of any printable ASCII characters except &lsquo;=&rsquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].value">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].value</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Variable references $(VAR_NAME) are expanded
using the previously defined environment variables in the container and
any service environment variables. If a variable cannot be resolved,
the reference in the input string will be unchanged. Double $$ are reduced
to a single $, which allows for escaping the $(VAR_NAME) syntax: i.e.
&ldquo;$$(VAR_NAME)&rdquo; will produce the string literal &ldquo;$(VAR_NAME)&rdquo;.
Escaped references will never be expanded, regardless of whether the variable
exists or not.
Defaults to &ldquo;&rdquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Source for the environment variable&rsquo;s value. Cannot be used if value is not empty.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.configMapKeyRef">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.configMapKeyRef</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Selects a key of a ConfigMap.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.configMapKeyRef.key">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.configMapKeyRef.key</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The key to select.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.configMapKeyRef.name">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.configMapKeyRef.name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.configMapKeyRef.optional">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.configMapKeyRef.optional</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Specify whether the ConfigMap or its key must be defined</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fieldRef">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fieldRef</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Selects a field of the pod: supports metadata.name, metadata.namespace, <code>metadata.labels['&lt;KEY&gt;']</code>, <code>metadata.annotations['&lt;KEY&gt;']</code>,
spec.nodeName, spec.serviceAccountName, status.hostIP, status.podIP, status.podIPs.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fieldRef.apiVersion">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fieldRef.apiVersion</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Version of the schema the FieldPath is written in terms of, defaults to &ldquo;v1&rdquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fieldRef.fieldPath">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fieldRef.fieldPath</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Path of the field to select in the specified API version.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fileKeyRef">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fileKeyRef</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>FileKeyRef selects a key of the env file.
Requires the EnvFiles feature gate to be enabled.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fileKeyRef.key">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fileKeyRef.key</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The key within the env file. An invalid key will prevent the pod from starting.
The keys defined within a source may consist of any printable ASCII characters except &lsquo;=&rsquo;.
During Alpha stage of the EnvFiles feature gate, the key size is limited to 128 characters.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fileKeyRef.optional">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fileKeyRef.optional</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Specify whether the file or its key must be defined. If the file or key
does not exist, then the env var is not published.
If optional is set to true and the specified key does not exist,
the environment variable will not be set in the Pod&rsquo;s containers.</p>

<p>If optional is set to false and the specified key does not exist,
an error will be returned during Pod creation.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fileKeyRef.path">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fileKeyRef.path</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The path within the volume from which to select the file.
Must be relative and may not contain the &lsquo;..&rsquo; path or start with &lsquo;..&rsquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fileKeyRef.volumeName">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fileKeyRef.volumeName</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The name of the volume mount containing the env file.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.resourceFieldRef">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.resourceFieldRef</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Selects a resource of the container: only resources limits and requests
(limits.cpu, limits.memory, limits.ephemeral-storage, requests.cpu, requests.memory and requests.ephemeral-storage) are currently supported.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.resourceFieldRef.containerName">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.resourceFieldRef.containerName</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Container name: required for volumes, optional for env vars</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.resourceFieldRef.divisor">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.resourceFieldRef.divisor</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">


</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Specifies the output format of the exposed resources, defaults to &ldquo;1&rdquo;</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.resourceFieldRef.resource">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.resourceFieldRef.resource</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Required: resource to select</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.secretKeyRef">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.secretKeyRef</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Selects a key of a secret in the pod&rsquo;s namespace</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.secretKeyRef.key">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.secretKeyRef.key</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The key of the secret to select from.  Must be a valid secret key.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.secretKeyRef.name">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.secretKeyRef.name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.secretKeyRef.optional">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].env[*].valueFrom.secretKeyRef.optional</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Specify whether the Secret or its key must be defined</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].image">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].image</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Container image name.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images</a>
This field is optional to allow higher level config management to default or override
container images in workload controllers like Deployments and StatefulSets.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].imagePullPolicy">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].imagePullPolicy</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Image pull policy.
One of Always, Never, IfNotPresent.
Defaults to Always if :latest tag is specified, or IfNotPresent otherwise.
Cannot be updated.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images#updating-images">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images#updating-images</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Actions that the management system should take in response to container lifecycle events.
Cannot be updated.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>PostStart is called immediately after a container is created. If the handler fails,
the container is terminated and restarted according to its restart policy.
Other management of the container blocks until the hook completes.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/container-lifecycle-hooks/#container-hooks">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/container-lifecycle-hooks/#container-hooks</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.exec">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.exec</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Exec specifies a command to execute in the container.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.exec.command">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.exec.command</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Command is the command line to execute inside the container, the working directory for the
command  is root (&lsquo;/&rsquo;) in the container&rsquo;s filesystem. The command is simply exec&rsquo;d, it is
not run inside a shell, so traditional shell instructions (&lsquo;|&rsquo;, etc) won&rsquo;t work. To use
a shell, you need to explicitly call out to that shell.
Exit status of 0 is treated as live/healthy and non-zero is unhealthy.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.exec.command[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.exec.command[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>HTTPGet specifies an HTTP GET request to perform.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.host">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.host</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Host name to connect to, defaults to the pod IP. You probably want to set
&ldquo;Host&rdquo; in httpHeaders instead.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.httpHeaders">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.httpHeaders</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Custom headers to set in the request. HTTP allows repeated headers.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.httpHeaders[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.httpHeaders[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>HTTPHeader describes a custom header to be used in HTTP probes</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-11">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.httpHeaders[*].name">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.httpHeaders[*].name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The header field name.
This will be canonicalized upon output, so case-variant names will be understood as the same header.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-11">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.httpHeaders[*].value">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.httpHeaders[*].value</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The header field value</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.path">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.path</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Path to access on the HTTP server.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.port">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.port</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">

<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name or number of the port to access on the container.
Number must be in the range 1 to 65535.
Name must be an IANA_SVC_NAME.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.scheme">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.scheme</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Scheme to use for connecting to the host.
Defaults to HTTP.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.sleep">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.sleep</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Sleep represents a duration that the container should sleep.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.sleep.seconds">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.sleep.seconds</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Seconds is the number of seconds to sleep.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.tcpSocket">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.tcpSocket</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Deprecated. TCPSocket is NOT supported as a LifecycleHandler and kept
for backward compatibility. There is no validation of this field and
lifecycle hooks will fail at runtime when it is specified.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.tcpSocket.host">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.tcpSocket.host</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Optional: Host name to connect to, defaults to the pod IP.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.tcpSocket.port">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.postStart.tcpSocket.port</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">

<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Number or name of the port to access on the container.
Number must be in the range 1 to 65535.
Name must be an IANA_SVC_NAME.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>PreStop is called immediately before a container is terminated due to an
API request or management event such as liveness/startup probe failure,
preemption, resource contention, etc. The handler is not called if the
container crashes or exits. The Pod&rsquo;s termination grace period countdown begins before the
PreStop hook is executed. Regardless of the outcome of the handler, the
container will eventually terminate within the Pod&rsquo;s termination grace
period (unless delayed by finalizers). Other management of the container blocks until the hook completes
or until the termination grace period is reached.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/container-lifecycle-hooks/#container-hooks">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/container-lifecycle-hooks/#container-hooks</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.exec">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.exec</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Exec specifies a command to execute in the container.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.exec.command">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.exec.command</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Command is the command line to execute inside the container, the working directory for the
command  is root (&lsquo;/&rsquo;) in the container&rsquo;s filesystem. The command is simply exec&rsquo;d, it is
not run inside a shell, so traditional shell instructions (&lsquo;|&rsquo;, etc) won&rsquo;t work. To use
a shell, you need to explicitly call out to that shell.
Exit status of 0 is treated as live/healthy and non-zero is unhealthy.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.exec.command[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.exec.command[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.httpGet">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.httpGet</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>HTTPGet specifies an HTTP GET request to perform.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.httpGet.host">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.httpGet.host</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Host name to connect to, defaults to the pod IP. You probably want to set
&ldquo;Host&rdquo; in httpHeaders instead.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.httpGet.httpHeaders">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.httpGet.httpHeaders</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Custom headers to set in the request. HTTP allows repeated headers.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.httpGet.httpHeaders[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.httpGet.httpHeaders[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>HTTPHeader describes a custom header to be used in HTTP probes</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-11">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.httpGet.httpHeaders[*].name">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.httpGet.httpHeaders[*].name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The header field name.
This will be canonicalized upon output, so case-variant names will be understood as the same header.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-11">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.httpGet.httpHeaders[*].value">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.httpGet.httpHeaders[*].value</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The header field value</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.httpGet.path">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.httpGet.path</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Path to access on the HTTP server.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.httpGet.port">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.httpGet.port</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">

<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name or number of the port to access on the container.
Number must be in the range 1 to 65535.
Name must be an IANA_SVC_NAME.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.httpGet.scheme">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.httpGet.scheme</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Scheme to use for connecting to the host.
Defaults to HTTP.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.sleep">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.sleep</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Sleep represents a duration that the container should sleep.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.sleep.seconds">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.sleep.seconds</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Seconds is the number of seconds to sleep.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.tcpSocket">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.tcpSocket</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Deprecated. TCPSocket is NOT supported as a LifecycleHandler and kept
for backward compatibility. There is no validation of this field and
lifecycle hooks will fail at runtime when it is specified.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.tcpSocket.host">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.tcpSocket.host</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Optional: Host name to connect to, defaults to the pod IP.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.tcpSocket.port">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.preStop.tcpSocket.port</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">

<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Number or name of the port to access on the container.
Number must be in the range 1 to 65535.
Name must be an IANA_SVC_NAME.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.stopSignal">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].lifecycle.stopSignal</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>StopSignal defines which signal will be sent to a container when it is being stopped.
If not specified, the default is defined by the container runtime in use.
StopSignal can only be set for Pods with a non-empty .spec.os.name</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].livenessProbe">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].livenessProbe</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Periodic probe of container liveness.
Container will be restarted if the probe fails.
Cannot be updated.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].livenessProbe.exec">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].livenessProbe.exec</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Exec specifies a command to execute in the container.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].livenessProbe.exec.command">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].livenessProbe.exec.command</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Command is the command line to execute inside the container, the working directory for the
command  is root (&lsquo;/&rsquo;) in the container&rsquo;s filesystem. The command is simply exec&rsquo;d, it is
not run inside a shell, so traditional shell instructions (&lsquo;|&rsquo;, etc) won&rsquo;t work. To use
a shell, you need to explicitly call out to that shell.
Exit status of 0 is treated as live/healthy and non-zero is unhealthy.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].livenessProbe.exec.command[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].livenessProbe.exec.command[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].livenessProbe.failureThreshold">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].livenessProbe.failureThreshold</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Minimum consecutive failures for the probe to be considered failed after having succeeded.
Defaults to 3. Minimum value is 1.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].livenessProbe.grpc">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].livenessProbe.grpc</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>GRPC specifies a GRPC HealthCheckRequest.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].livenessProbe.grpc.port">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].livenessProbe.grpc.port</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Port number of the gRPC service. Number must be in the range 1 to 65535.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].livenessProbe.grpc.service">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.containers[*].livenessProbe.grpc.service</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Service is the name of the service to place in the gRPC HealthCheckRequest
(see <a href="https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/doc/health-checking.md">https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/doc/health-checking.md</a>).</p>

<p>If this is not specified, the default behavior is defined by gRPC.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
