<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The header field value</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.httpGet.path">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.httpGet.path</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Path to access on the HTTP server.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.httpGet.port">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.httpGet.port</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">

<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name or number of the port to access on the container.
Number must be in the range 1 to 65535.
Name must be an IANA_SVC_NAME.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.httpGet.scheme">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.httpGet.scheme</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Scheme to use for connecting to the host.
Defaults to HTTP.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.initialDelaySeconds">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.initialDelaySeconds</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Number of seconds after the container has started before liveness probes are initiated.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.periodSeconds">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.periodSeconds</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>How often (in seconds) to perform the probe.
Default to 10 seconds. Minimum value is 1.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.successThreshold">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.successThreshold</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Minimum consecutive successes for the probe to be considered successful after having failed.
Defaults to 1. Must be 1 for liveness and startup. Minimum value is 1.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.tcpSocket">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.tcpSocket</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>TCPSocket specifies a connection to a TCP port.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.tcpSocket.host">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.tcpSocket.host</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Optional: Host name to connect to, defaults to the pod IP.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.tcpSocket.port">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.tcpSocket.port</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">

<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Number or name of the port to access on the container.
Number must be in the range 1 to 65535.
Name must be an IANA_SVC_NAME.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Optional duration in seconds the pod needs to terminate gracefully upon probe failure.
The grace period is the duration in seconds after the processes running in the pod are sent
a termination signal and the time when the processes are forcibly halted with a kill signal.
Set this value longer than the expected cleanup time for your process.
If this value is nil, the pod&rsquo;s terminationGracePeriodSeconds will be used. Otherwise, this
value overrides the value provided by the pod spec.
Value must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via
the kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).
This is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.
Minimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.timeoutSeconds">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.timeoutSeconds</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Number of seconds after which the probe times out.
Defaults to 1 second. Minimum value is 1.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].name">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name of the container specified as a DNS_LABEL.
Each container in a pod must have a unique name (DNS_LABEL).
Cannot be updated.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].ports">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].ports</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>List of ports to expose from the container. Not specifying a port here
DOES NOT prevent that port from being exposed. Any port which is
listening on the default &ldquo;0.0.0.0&rdquo; address inside a container will be
accessible from the network.
Modifying this array with strategic merge patch may corrupt the data.
For more information See <a href="https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/108255">https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/108255</a>.
Cannot be updated.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].ports[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].ports[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>ContainerPort represents a network port in a single container.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].ports[*].containerPort">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].ports[*].containerPort</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Number of port to expose on the pod&rsquo;s IP address.
This must be a valid port number, 0 &lt; x &lt; 65536.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].ports[*].hostIP">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].ports[*].hostIP</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>What host IP to bind the external port to.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].ports[*].hostPort">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].ports[*].hostPort</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Number of port to expose on the host.
If specified, this must be a valid port number, 0 &lt; x &lt; 65536.
If HostNetwork is specified, this must match ContainerPort.
Most containers do not need this.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].ports[*].name">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].ports[*].name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>If specified, this must be an IANA_SVC_NAME and unique within the pod. Each
named port in a pod must have a unique name. Name for the port that can be
referred to by services.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].ports[*].protocol">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].ports[*].protocol</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Protocol for port. Must be UDP, TCP, or SCTP.
Defaults to &ldquo;TCP&rdquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Periodic probe of container service readiness.
Container will be removed from service endpoints if the probe fails.
Cannot be updated.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.exec">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.exec</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Exec specifies a command to execute in the container.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.exec.command">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.exec.command</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Command is the command line to execute inside the container, the working directory for the
command  is root (&lsquo;/&rsquo;) in the container&rsquo;s filesystem. The command is simply exec&rsquo;d, it is
not run inside a shell, so traditional shell instructions (&lsquo;|&rsquo;, etc) won&rsquo;t work. To use
a shell, you need to explicitly call out to that shell.
Exit status of 0 is treated as live/healthy and non-zero is unhealthy.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.exec.command[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.exec.command[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.failureThreshold">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.failureThreshold</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Minimum consecutive failures for the probe to be considered failed after having succeeded.
Defaults to 3. Minimum value is 1.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.grpc">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.grpc</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>GRPC specifies a GRPC HealthCheckRequest.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.grpc.port">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.grpc.port</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Port number of the gRPC service. Number must be in the range 1 to 65535.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.grpc.service">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.grpc.service</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Service is the name of the service to place in the gRPC HealthCheckRequest
(see <a href="https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/doc/health-checking.md">https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/doc/health-checking.md</a>).</p>

<p>If this is not specified, the default behavior is defined by gRPC.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>HTTPGet specifies an HTTP GET request to perform.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.host">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.host</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Host name to connect to, defaults to the pod IP. You probably want to set
&ldquo;Host&rdquo; in httpHeaders instead.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Custom headers to set in the request. HTTP allows repeated headers.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>HTTPHeader describes a custom header to be used in HTTP probes</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders[*].name">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders[*].name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The header field name.
This will be canonicalized upon output, so case-variant names will be understood as the same header.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders[*].value">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders[*].value</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The header field value</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.path">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.path</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Path to access on the HTTP server.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.port">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.port</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">

<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name or number of the port to access on the container.
Number must be in the range 1 to 65535.
Name must be an IANA_SVC_NAME.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.scheme">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.scheme</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Scheme to use for connecting to the host.
Defaults to HTTP.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.initialDelaySeconds">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.initialDelaySeconds</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Number of seconds after the container has started before liveness probes are initiated.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.periodSeconds">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.periodSeconds</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>How often (in seconds) to perform the probe.
Default to 10 seconds. Minimum value is 1.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.successThreshold">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.successThreshold</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Minimum consecutive successes for the probe to be considered successful after having failed.
Defaults to 1. Must be 1 for liveness and startup. Minimum value is 1.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.tcpSocket">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.tcpSocket</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>TCPSocket specifies a connection to a TCP port.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.tcpSocket.host">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.tcpSocket.host</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Optional: Host name to connect to, defaults to the pod IP.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.tcpSocket.port">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.tcpSocket.port</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">

<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Number or name of the port to access on the container.
Number must be in the range 1 to 65535.
Name must be an IANA_SVC_NAME.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Optional duration in seconds the pod needs to terminate gracefully upon probe failure.
The grace period is the duration in seconds after the processes running in the pod are sent
a termination signal and the time when the processes are forcibly halted with a kill signal.
Set this value longer than the expected cleanup time for your process.
If this value is nil, the pod&rsquo;s terminationGracePeriodSeconds will be used. Otherwise, this
value overrides the value provided by the pod spec.
Value must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via
the kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).
This is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.
Minimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.timeoutSeconds">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.timeoutSeconds</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Number of seconds after which the probe times out.
Defaults to 1 second. Minimum value is 1.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].resizePolicy">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].resizePolicy</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Resources resize policy for the container.
This field cannot be set on ephemeral containers.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].resizePolicy[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].resizePolicy[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>ContainerResizePolicy represents resource resize policy for the container.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].resizePolicy[*].resourceName">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].resizePolicy[*].resourceName</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name of the resource to which this resource resize policy applies.
Supported values: cpu, memory.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].resizePolicy[*].restartPolicy">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].resizePolicy[*].restartPolicy</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Restart policy to apply when specified resource is resized.
If not specified, it defaults to NotRequired.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].resources">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].resources</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Compute Resources required by this container.
Cannot be updated.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].resources.claims">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].resources.claims</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Claims lists the names of resources, defined in spec.resourceClaims,
that are used by this container.</p>

<p>This field depends on the
DynamicResourceAllocation feature gate.</p>

<p>This field is immutable. It can only be set for containers.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].resources.claims[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].resources.claims[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>ResourceClaim references one entry in PodSpec.ResourceClaims.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].resources.claims[*].name">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].resources.claims[*].name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name must match the name of one entry in pod.spec.resourceClaims of
the Pod where this field is used. It makes that resource available
inside a container.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].resources.claims[*].request">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].resources.claims[*].request</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Request is the name chosen for a request in the referenced claim.
If empty, everything from the claim is made available, otherwise
only the result of this request.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].resources.limits">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].resources.limits</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Limits describes the maximum amount of compute resources allowed.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].resources.requests">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].resources.requests</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Requests describes the minimum amount of compute resources required.
If Requests is omitted for a container, it defaults to Limits if that is explicitly specified,
otherwise to an implementation-defined value. Requests cannot exceed Limits.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].restartPolicy">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].restartPolicy</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>RestartPolicy defines the restart behavior of individual containers in a pod.
This overrides the pod-level restart policy. When this field is not specified,
the restart behavior is defined by the Pod&rsquo;s restart policy and the container type.
Additionally, setting the RestartPolicy as &ldquo;Always&rdquo; for the init container will
have the following effect:
this init container will be continually restarted on
exit until all regular containers have terminated. Once all regular
containers have completed, all init containers with restartPolicy &ldquo;Always&rdquo;
will be shut down. This lifecycle differs from normal init containers and
is often referred to as a &ldquo;sidecar&rdquo; container. Although this init
container still starts in the init container sequence, it does not wait
for the container to complete before proceeding to the next init
container. Instead, the next init container starts immediately after this
init container is started, or after any startupProbe has successfully
completed.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].restartPolicyRules">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].restartPolicyRules</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Represents a list of rules to be checked to determine if the
container should be restarted on exit. The rules are evaluated in
order. Once a rule matches a container exit condition, the remaining
rules are ignored. If no rule matches the container exit condition,
the Container-level restart policy determines the whether the container
is restarted or not. Constraints on the rules:
- At most 20 rules are allowed.
- Rules can have the same action.
- Identical rules are not forbidden in validations.
When rules are specified, container MUST set RestartPolicy explicitly
even it if matches the Pod&rsquo;s RestartPolicy.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].restartPolicyRules[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].restartPolicyRules[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>ContainerRestartRule describes how a container exit is handled.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].restartPolicyRules[*].action">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].restartPolicyRules[*].action</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Specifies the action taken on a container exit if the requirements
are satisfied. The only possible value is &ldquo;Restart&rdquo; to restart the
container.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].restartPolicyRules[*].exitCodes">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].restartPolicyRules[*].exitCodes</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Represents the exit codes to check on container exits.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].restartPolicyRules[*].exitCodes.operator">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].restartPolicyRules[*].exitCodes.operator</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Represents the relationship between the container exit code(s) and the
specified values. Possible values are:
- In: the requirement is satisfied if the container exit code is in the
  set of specified values.
- NotIn: the requirement is satisfied if the container exit code is
  not in the set of specified values.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].restartPolicyRules[*].exitCodes.values">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].restartPolicyRules[*].exitCodes.values</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Specifies the set of values to check for container exit codes.
At most 255 elements are allowed.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].restartPolicyRules[*].exitCodes.values[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].restartPolicyRules[*].exitCodes.values[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>SecurityContext defines the security options the container should be run with.
If set, the fields of SecurityContext override the equivalent fields of PodSecurityContext.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/">https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.allowPrivilegeEscalation">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.allowPrivilegeEscalation</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>AllowPrivilegeEscalation controls whether a process can gain more
privileges than its parent process. This bool directly controls if
the no_new_privs flag will be set on the container process.
AllowPrivilegeEscalation is true always when the container is:
1) run as Privileged
2) has CAP_SYS_ADMIN
Note that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.appArmorProfile">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.appArmorProfile</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>appArmorProfile is the AppArmor options to use by this container. If set, this profile
overrides the pod&rsquo;s appArmorProfile.
Note that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.appArmorProfile.localhostProfile">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.appArmorProfile.localhostProfile</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>localhostProfile indicates a profile loaded on the node that should be used.
The profile must be preconfigured on the node to work.
Must match the loaded name of the profile.
Must be set if and only if type is &ldquo;Localhost&rdquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.appArmorProfile.type">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.appArmorProfile.type</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>type indicates which kind of AppArmor profile will be applied.
Valid options are:
  Localhost - a profile pre-loaded on the node.
  RuntimeDefault - the container runtime&rsquo;s default profile.
  Unconfined - no AppArmor enforcement.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.capabilities">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.capabilities</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The capabilities to add/drop when running containers.
Defaults to the default set of capabilities granted by the container runtime.
Note that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.capabilities.add">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.capabilities.add</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Added capabilities</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.capabilities.add[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.capabilities.add[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Capability represent POSIX capabilities type</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.capabilities.drop">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.capabilities.drop</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Removed capabilities</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.capabilities.drop[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.capabilities.drop[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Capability represent POSIX capabilities type</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.privileged">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.privileged</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Run container in privileged mode.
Processes in privileged containers are essentially equivalent to root on the host.
Defaults to false.
Note that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.procMount">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.procMount</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>procMount denotes the type of proc mount to use for the containers.
The default value is Default which uses the container runtime defaults for
readonly paths and masked paths.
Note that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Whether this container has a read-only root filesystem.
Default is false.
Note that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.runAsGroup">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.runAsGroup</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The GID to run the entrypoint of the container process.
Uses runtime default if unset.
May also be set in PodSecurityContext.  If set in both SecurityContext and
PodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext takes precedence.
Note that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.runAsNonRoot">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.runAsNonRoot</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Indicates that the container must run as a non-root user.
If true, the Kubelet will validate the image at runtime to ensure that it
does not run as UID 0 (root) and fail to start the container if it does.
If unset or false, no such validation will be performed.
May also be set in PodSecurityContext.  If set in both SecurityContext and
PodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext takes precedence.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.runAsUser">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.runAsUser</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The UID to run the entrypoint of the container process.
Defaults to user specified in image metadata if unspecified.
May also be set in PodSecurityContext.  If set in both SecurityContext and
PodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext takes precedence.
Note that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.seLinuxOptions">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.seLinuxOptions</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The SELinux context to be applied to the container.
If unspecified, the container runtime will allocate a random SELinux context for each
container.  May also be set in PodSecurityContext.  If set in both SecurityContext and
PodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext takes precedence.
Note that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.seLinuxOptions.level">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.seLinuxOptions.level</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Level is SELinux level label that applies to the container.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.seLinuxOptions.role">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.seLinuxOptions.role</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Role is a SELinux role label that applies to the container.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.seLinuxOptions.type">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.seLinuxOptions.type</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Type is a SELinux type label that applies to the container.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.seLinuxOptions.user">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.seLinuxOptions.user</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>User is a SELinux user label that applies to the container.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.seccompProfile">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.seccompProfile</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The seccomp options to use by this container. If seccomp options are
provided at both the pod &amp; container level, the container options
override the pod options.
Note that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.seccompProfile.localhostProfile">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.seccompProfile.localhostProfile</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>localhostProfile indicates a profile defined in a file on the node should be used.
The profile must be preconfigured on the node to work.
Must be a descending path, relative to the kubelet&rsquo;s configured seccomp profile location.
Must be set if type is &ldquo;Localhost&rdquo;. Must NOT be set for any other type.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.seccompProfile.type">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.seccompProfile.type</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>type indicates which kind of seccomp profile will be applied.
Valid options are:</p>

<p>Localhost - a profile defined in a file on the node should be used.
RuntimeDefault - the container runtime default profile should be used.
Unconfined - no profile should be applied.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.windowsOptions">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.windowsOptions</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The Windows specific settings applied to all containers.
If unspecified, the options from the PodSecurityContext will be used.
If set in both SecurityContext and PodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext takes precedence.
Note that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is linux.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.windowsOptions.gmsaCredentialSpec">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.windowsOptions.gmsaCredentialSpec</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>GMSACredentialSpec is where the GMSA admission webhook
(<a href="https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/windows-gmsa">https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/windows-gmsa</a>) inlines the contents of the
GMSA credential spec named by the GMSACredentialSpecName field.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.windowsOptions.gmsaCredentialSpecName">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.windowsOptions.gmsaCredentialSpecName</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>GMSACredentialSpecName is the name of the GMSA credential spec to use.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.windowsOptions.hostProcess">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.windowsOptions.hostProcess</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>HostProcess determines if a container should be run as a &lsquo;Host Process&rsquo; container.
All of a Pod&rsquo;s containers must have the same effective HostProcess value
(it is not allowed to have a mix of HostProcess containers and non-HostProcess containers).
In addition, if HostProcess is true then HostNetwork must also be set to true.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.windowsOptions.runAsUserName">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].securityContext.windowsOptions.runAsUserName</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The UserName in Windows to run the entrypoint of the container process.
Defaults to the user specified in image metadata if unspecified.
May also be set in PodSecurityContext. If set in both SecurityContext and
PodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext takes precedence.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>StartupProbe indicates that the Pod has successfully initialized.
If specified, no other probes are executed until this completes successfully.
If this probe fails, the Pod will be restarted, just as if the livenessProbe failed.
This can be used to provide different probe parameters at the beginning of a Pod&rsquo;s lifecycle,
when it might take a long time to load data or warm a cache, than during steady-state operation.
This cannot be updated.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.exec">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.exec</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Exec specifies a command to execute in the container.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.exec.command">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.exec.command</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Command is the command line to execute inside the container, the working directory for the
command  is root (&lsquo;/&rsquo;) in the container&rsquo;s filesystem. The command is simply exec&rsquo;d, it is
not run inside a shell, so traditional shell instructions (&lsquo;|&rsquo;, etc) won&rsquo;t work. To use
a shell, you need to explicitly call out to that shell.
Exit status of 0 is treated as live/healthy and non-zero is unhealthy.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.exec.command[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.exec.command[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.failureThreshold">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.failureThreshold</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Minimum consecutive failures for the probe to be considered failed after having succeeded.
Defaults to 3. Minimum value is 1.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.grpc">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.grpc</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>GRPC specifies a GRPC HealthCheckRequest.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.grpc.port">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.grpc.port</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Port number of the gRPC service. Number must be in the range 1 to 65535.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.grpc.service">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.grpc.service</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Service is the name of the service to place in the gRPC HealthCheckRequest
(see <a href="https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/doc/health-checking.md">https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/doc/health-checking.md</a>).</p>

<p>If this is not specified, the default behavior is defined by gRPC.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.httpGet">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.httpGet</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>HTTPGet specifies an HTTP GET request to perform.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.httpGet.host">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.httpGet.host</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Host name to connect to, defaults to the pod IP. You probably want to set
&ldquo;Host&rdquo; in httpHeaders instead.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Custom headers to set in the request. HTTP allows repeated headers.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>HTTPHeader describes a custom header to be used in HTTP probes</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders[*].name">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders[*].name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The header field name.
This will be canonicalized upon output, so case-variant names will be understood as the same header.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders[*].value">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders[*].value</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The header field value</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.httpGet.path">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.httpGet.path</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Path to access on the HTTP server.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.httpGet.port">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.httpGet.port</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">

<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name or number of the port to access on the container.
Number must be in the range 1 to 65535.
Name must be an IANA_SVC_NAME.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.httpGet.scheme">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.httpGet.scheme</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Scheme to use for connecting to the host.
Defaults to HTTP.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.initialDelaySeconds">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.initialDelaySeconds</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Number of seconds after the container has started before liveness probes are initiated.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.periodSeconds">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.periodSeconds</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>How often (in seconds) to perform the probe.
Default to 10 seconds. Minimum value is 1.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.successThreshold">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.successThreshold</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Minimum consecutive successes for the probe to be considered successful after having failed.
Defaults to 1. Must be 1 for liveness and startup. Minimum value is 1.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.tcpSocket">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.tcpSocket</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>TCPSocket specifies a connection to a TCP port.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.tcpSocket.host">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.tcpSocket.host</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Optional: Host name to connect to, defaults to the pod IP.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.tcpSocket.port">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.tcpSocket.port</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">

<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Number or name of the port to access on the container.
Number must be in the range 1 to 65535.
Name must be an IANA_SVC_NAME.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Optional duration in seconds the pod needs to terminate gracefully upon probe failure.
The grace period is the duration in seconds after the processes running in the pod are sent
a termination signal and the time when the processes are forcibly halted with a kill signal.
Set this value longer than the expected cleanup time for your process.
If this value is nil, the pod&rsquo;s terminationGracePeriodSeconds will be used. Otherwise, this
value overrides the value provided by the pod spec.
Value must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via
the kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).
This is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.
Minimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.timeoutSeconds">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].startupProbe.timeoutSeconds</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Number of seconds after which the probe times out.
Defaults to 1 second. Minimum value is 1.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].stdin">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].stdin</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Whether this container should allocate a buffer for stdin in the container runtime. If this
is not set, reads from stdin in the container will always result in EOF.
Default is false.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].stdinOnce">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].stdinOnce</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Whether the container runtime should close the stdin channel after it has been opened by
a single attach. When stdin is true the stdin stream will remain open across multiple attach
sessions. If stdinOnce is set to true, stdin is opened on container start, is empty until the
first client attaches to stdin, and then remains open and accepts data until the client disconnects,
at which time stdin is closed and remains closed until the container is restarted. If this
flag is false, a container processes that reads from stdin will never receive an EOF.
Default is false</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].terminationMessagePath">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].terminationMessagePath</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Optional: Path at which the file to which the container&rsquo;s termination message
will be written is mounted into the container&rsquo;s filesystem.
Message written is intended to be brief final status, such as an assertion failure message.
Will be truncated by the node if greater than 4096 bytes. The total message length across
all containers will be limited to 12kb.
Defaults to /dev/termination-log.
Cannot be updated.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].terminationMessagePolicy">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].terminationMessagePolicy</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Indicate how the termination message should be populated. File will use the contents of
terminationMessagePath to populate the container status message on both success and failure.
FallbackToLogsOnError will use the last chunk of container log output if the termination
message file is empty and the container exited with an error.
The log output is limited to 2048 bytes or 80 lines, whichever is smaller.
Defaults to File.
Cannot be updated.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].tty">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].tty</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Whether this container should allocate a TTY for itself, also requires &lsquo;stdin&rsquo; to be true.
Default is false.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].volumeDevices">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].volumeDevices</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>volumeDevices is the list of block devices to be used by the container.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].volumeDevices[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].volumeDevices[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>volumeDevice describes a mapping of a raw block device within a container.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].volumeDevices[*].devicePath">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].volumeDevices[*].devicePath</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>devicePath is the path inside of the container that the device will be mapped to.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].volumeDevices[*].name">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].volumeDevices[*].name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>name must match the name of a persistentVolumeClaim in the pod</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].volumeMounts">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.initContainers[*].volumeMounts</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
