<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.tolerations">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.tolerations</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.tolerations[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.tolerations[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The pod this Toleration is attached to tolerates any taint that matches
the triple <key,value,effect> using the matching operator <operator>.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.tolerations[*].effect">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.tolerations[*].effect</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Effect indicates the taint effect to match. Empty means match all taint effects.
When specified, allowed values are NoSchedule, PreferNoSchedule and NoExecute.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.tolerations[*].key">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.tolerations[*].key</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Key is the taint key that the toleration applies to. Empty means match all taint keys.
If the key is empty, operator must be Exists; this combination means to match all values and all keys.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.tolerations[*].operator">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.tolerations[*].operator</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Operator represents a key&rsquo;s relationship to the value.
Valid operators are Exists, Equal, Lt, and Gt. Defaults to Equal.
Exists is equivalent to wildcard for value, so that a pod can
tolerate all taints of a particular category.
Lt and Gt perform numeric comparisons (requires feature gate TaintTolerationComparisonOperators).</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.tolerations[*].tolerationSeconds">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.tolerations[*].tolerationSeconds</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>TolerationSeconds represents the period of time the toleration (which must be
of effect NoExecute, otherwise this field is ignored) tolerates the taint. By default,
it is not set, which means tolerate the taint forever (do not evict). Zero and
negative values will be treated as 0 (evict immediately) by the system.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.tolerations[*].value">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.tolerations[*].value</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Value is the taint value the toleration matches to.
If the operator is Exists, the value should be empty, otherwise just a regular string.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumeMounts">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumeMounts</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>VolumeMounts define some extra Kubernetes VolumeMounts for the Kafka broker Pods.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumeMounts[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumeMounts[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>VolumeMount describes a mounting of a Volume within a container.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumeMounts[*].mountPath">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumeMounts[*].mountPath</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Path within the container at which the volume should be mounted.  Must
not contain &lsquo;:&rsquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumeMounts[*].mountPropagation">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumeMounts[*].mountPropagation</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>mountPropagation determines how mounts are propagated from the host
to container and the other way around.
When not set, MountPropagationNone is used.
This field is beta in 1.10.
When RecursiveReadOnly is set to IfPossible or to Enabled, MountPropagation must be None or unspecified
(which defaults to None).</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumeMounts[*].name">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumeMounts[*].name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>This must match the Name of a Volume.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumeMounts[*].readOnly">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumeMounts[*].readOnly</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Mounted read-only if true, read-write otherwise (false or unspecified).
Defaults to false.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumeMounts[*].recursiveReadOnly">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumeMounts[*].recursiveReadOnly</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>RecursiveReadOnly specifies whether read-only mounts should be handled
recursively.</p>

<p>If ReadOnly is false, this field has no meaning and must be unspecified.</p>

<p>If ReadOnly is true, and this field is set to Disabled, the mount is not made
recursively read-only.  If this field is set to IfPossible, the mount is made
recursively read-only, if it is supported by the container runtime.  If this
field is set to Enabled, the mount is made recursively read-only if it is
supported by the container runtime, otherwise the pod will not be started and
an error will be generated to indicate the reason.</p>

<p>If this field is set to IfPossible or Enabled, MountPropagation must be set to
None (or be unspecified, which defaults to None).</p>

<p>If this field is not specified, it is treated as an equivalent of Disabled.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumeMounts[*].subPath">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumeMounts[*].subPath</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Path within the volume from which the container&rsquo;s volume should be mounted.
Defaults to &ldquo;&rdquo; (volume&rsquo;s root).</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumeMounts[*].subPathExpr">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumeMounts[*].subPathExpr</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Expanded path within the volume from which the container&rsquo;s volume should be mounted.
Behaves similarly to SubPath but environment variable references $(VAR_NAME) are expanded using the container&rsquo;s environment.
Defaults to &ldquo;&rdquo; (volume&rsquo;s root).
SubPathExpr and SubPath are mutually exclusive.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Volumes define some extra Kubernetes Volumes for the Kafka broker Pods.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Volume represents a named volume in a pod that may be accessed by any container in the pod.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].awsElasticBlockStore">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].awsElasticBlockStore</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>awsElasticBlockStore represents an AWS Disk resource that is attached to a
kubelet&rsquo;s host machine and then exposed to the pod.
Deprecated: AWSElasticBlockStore is deprecated. All operations for the in-tree
awsElasticBlockStore type are redirected to the ebs.csi.aws.com CSI driver.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#awselasticblockstore">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#awselasticblockstore</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].awsElasticBlockStore.fsType">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].awsElasticBlockStore.fsType</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>fsType is the filesystem type of the volume that you want to mount.
Tip: Ensure that the filesystem type is supported by the host operating system.
Examples: &ldquo;ext4&rdquo;, &ldquo;xfs&rdquo;, &ldquo;ntfs&rdquo;. Implicitly inferred to be &ldquo;ext4&rdquo; if unspecified.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#awselasticblockstore">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#awselasticblockstore</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].awsElasticBlockStore.partition">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].awsElasticBlockStore.partition</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>partition is the partition in the volume that you want to mount.
If omitted, the default is to mount by volume name.
Examples: For volume /dev/sda1, you specify the partition as &ldquo;1&rdquo;.
Similarly, the volume partition for /dev/sda is &ldquo;0&rdquo; (or you can leave the property empty).</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].awsElasticBlockStore.readOnly">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].awsElasticBlockStore.readOnly</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>readOnly value true will force the readOnly setting in VolumeMounts.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#awselasticblockstore">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#awselasticblockstore</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].awsElasticBlockStore.volumeID">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].awsElasticBlockStore.volumeID</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>volumeID is unique ID of the persistent disk resource in AWS (Amazon EBS volume).
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#awselasticblockstore">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#awselasticblockstore</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].azureDisk">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].azureDisk</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>azureDisk represents an Azure Data Disk mount on the host and bind mount to the pod.
Deprecated: AzureDisk is deprecated. All operations for the in-tree azureDisk type
are redirected to the disk.csi.azure.com CSI driver.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].azureDisk.cachingMode">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].azureDisk.cachingMode</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>cachingMode is the Host Caching mode: None, Read Only, Read Write.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].azureDisk.diskName">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].azureDisk.diskName</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>diskName is the Name of the data disk in the blob storage</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].azureDisk.diskURI">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].azureDisk.diskURI</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>diskURI is the URI of data disk in the blob storage</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].azureDisk.fsType">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].azureDisk.fsType</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>fsType is Filesystem type to mount.
Must be a filesystem type supported by the host operating system.
Ex. &ldquo;ext4&rdquo;, &ldquo;xfs&rdquo;, &ldquo;ntfs&rdquo;. Implicitly inferred to be &ldquo;ext4&rdquo; if unspecified.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].azureDisk.kind">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].azureDisk.kind</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>kind expected values are Shared: multiple blob disks per storage account  Dedicated: single blob disk per storage account  Managed: azure managed data disk (only in managed availability set). defaults to shared</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].azureDisk.readOnly">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].azureDisk.readOnly</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>readOnly Defaults to false (read/write). ReadOnly here will force
the ReadOnly setting in VolumeMounts.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].azureFile">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].azureFile</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>azureFile represents an Azure File Service mount on the host and bind mount to the pod.
Deprecated: AzureFile is deprecated. All operations for the in-tree azureFile type
are redirected to the file.csi.azure.com CSI driver.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].azureFile.readOnly">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].azureFile.readOnly</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>readOnly defaults to false (read/write). ReadOnly here will force
the ReadOnly setting in VolumeMounts.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].azureFile.secretName">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].azureFile.secretName</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>secretName is the  name of secret that contains Azure Storage Account Name and Key</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].azureFile.shareName">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].azureFile.shareName</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>shareName is the azure share Name</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cephfs">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cephfs</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>cephFS represents a Ceph FS mount on the host that shares a pod&rsquo;s lifetime.
Deprecated: CephFS is deprecated and the in-tree cephfs type is no longer supported.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cephfs.monitors">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cephfs.monitors</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>monitors is Required: Monitors is a collection of Ceph monitors
More info: <a href="https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/cephfs/README.md#how-to-use-it">https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/cephfs/README.md#how-to-use-it</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cephfs.monitors[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cephfs.monitors[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cephfs.path">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cephfs.path</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>path is Optional: Used as the mounted root, rather than the full Ceph tree, default is /</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cephfs.readOnly">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cephfs.readOnly</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>readOnly is Optional: Defaults to false (read/write). ReadOnly here will force
the ReadOnly setting in VolumeMounts.
More info: <a href="https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/cephfs/README.md#how-to-use-it">https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/cephfs/README.md#how-to-use-it</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cephfs.secretFile">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cephfs.secretFile</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>secretFile is Optional: SecretFile is the path to key ring for User, default is /etc/ceph/user.secret
More info: <a href="https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/cephfs/README.md#how-to-use-it">https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/cephfs/README.md#how-to-use-it</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cephfs.secretRef">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cephfs.secretRef</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>secretRef is Optional: SecretRef is reference to the authentication secret for User, default is empty.
More info: <a href="https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/cephfs/README.md#how-to-use-it">https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/cephfs/README.md#how-to-use-it</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cephfs.secretRef.name">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cephfs.secretRef.name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cephfs.user">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cephfs.user</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>user is optional: User is the rados user name, default is admin
More info: <a href="https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/cephfs/README.md#how-to-use-it">https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/cephfs/README.md#how-to-use-it</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cinder">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cinder</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>cinder represents a cinder volume attached and mounted on kubelets host machine.
Deprecated: Cinder is deprecated. All operations for the in-tree cinder type
are redirected to the cinder.csi.openstack.org CSI driver.
More info: <a href="https://examples.k8s.io/mysql-cinder-pd/README.md">https://examples.k8s.io/mysql-cinder-pd/README.md</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cinder.fsType">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cinder.fsType</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>fsType is the filesystem type to mount.
Must be a filesystem type supported by the host operating system.
Examples: &ldquo;ext4&rdquo;, &ldquo;xfs&rdquo;, &ldquo;ntfs&rdquo;. Implicitly inferred to be &ldquo;ext4&rdquo; if unspecified.
More info: <a href="https://examples.k8s.io/mysql-cinder-pd/README.md">https://examples.k8s.io/mysql-cinder-pd/README.md</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cinder.readOnly">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cinder.readOnly</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>readOnly defaults to false (read/write). ReadOnly here will force
the ReadOnly setting in VolumeMounts.
More info: <a href="https://examples.k8s.io/mysql-cinder-pd/README.md">https://examples.k8s.io/mysql-cinder-pd/README.md</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cinder.secretRef">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cinder.secretRef</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>secretRef is optional: points to a secret object containing parameters used to connect
to OpenStack.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cinder.secretRef.name">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cinder.secretRef.name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cinder.volumeID">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].cinder.volumeID</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>volumeID used to identify the volume in cinder.
More info: <a href="https://examples.k8s.io/mysql-cinder-pd/README.md">https://examples.k8s.io/mysql-cinder-pd/README.md</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].configMap">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].configMap</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>configMap represents a configMap that should populate this volume</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].configMap.defaultMode">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].configMap.defaultMode</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>defaultMode is optional: mode bits used to set permissions on created files by default.
Must be an octal value between 0000 and 0777 or a decimal value between 0 and 511.
YAML accepts both octal and decimal values, JSON requires decimal values for mode bits.
Defaults to 0644.
Directories within the path are not affected by this setting.
This might be in conflict with other options that affect the file
mode, like fsGroup, and the result can be other mode bits set.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].configMap.items">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].configMap.items</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>items if unspecified, each key-value pair in the Data field of the referenced
ConfigMap will be projected into the volume as a file whose name is the
key and content is the value. If specified, the listed keys will be
projected into the specified paths, and unlisted keys will not be
present. If a key is specified which is not present in the ConfigMap,
the volume setup will error unless it is marked optional. Paths must be
relative and may not contain the &lsquo;..&rsquo; path or start with &lsquo;..&rsquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].configMap.items[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].configMap.items[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Maps a string key to a path within a volume.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].configMap.items[*].key">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].configMap.items[*].key</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>key is the key to project.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].configMap.items[*].mode">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].configMap.items[*].mode</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>mode is Optional: mode bits used to set permissions on this file.
Must be an octal value between 0000 and 0777 or a decimal value between 0 and 511.
YAML accepts both octal and decimal values, JSON requires decimal values for mode bits.
If not specified, the volume defaultMode will be used.
This might be in conflict with other options that affect the file
mode, like fsGroup, and the result can be other mode bits set.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].configMap.items[*].path">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].configMap.items[*].path</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>path is the relative path of the file to map the key to.
May not be an absolute path.
May not contain the path element &lsquo;..&rsquo;.
May not start with the string &lsquo;..&rsquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].configMap.name">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].configMap.name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].configMap.optional">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].configMap.optional</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>optional specify whether the ConfigMap or its keys must be defined</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].csi">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].csi</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>csi (Container Storage Interface) represents ephemeral storage that is handled by certain external CSI drivers.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].csi.driver">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].csi.driver</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>driver is the name of the CSI driver that handles this volume.
Consult with your admin for the correct name as registered in the cluster.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].csi.fsType">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].csi.fsType</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>fsType to mount. Ex. &ldquo;ext4&rdquo;, &ldquo;xfs&rdquo;, &ldquo;ntfs&rdquo;.
If not provided, the empty value is passed to the associated CSI driver
which will determine the default filesystem to apply.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].csi.nodePublishSecretRef">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].csi.nodePublishSecretRef</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>nodePublishSecretRef is a reference to the secret object containing
sensitive information to pass to the CSI driver to complete the CSI
NodePublishVolume and NodeUnpublishVolume calls.
This field is optional, and  may be empty if no secret is required. If the
secret object contains more than one secret, all secret references are passed.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].csi.nodePublishSecretRef.name">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].csi.nodePublishSecretRef.name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].csi.readOnly">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].csi.readOnly</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>readOnly specifies a read-only configuration for the volume.
Defaults to false (read/write).</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].csi.volumeAttributes">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].csi.volumeAttributes</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>volumeAttributes stores driver-specific properties that are passed to the CSI
driver. Consult your driver&rsquo;s documentation for supported values.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].downwardAPI">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].downwardAPI</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>downwardAPI represents downward API about the pod that should populate this volume</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].downwardAPI.defaultMode">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].downwardAPI.defaultMode</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Optional: mode bits to use on created files by default. Must be a
Optional: mode bits used to set permissions on created files by default.
Must be an octal value between 0000 and 0777 or a decimal value between 0 and 511.
YAML accepts both octal and decimal values, JSON requires decimal values for mode bits.
Defaults to 0644.
Directories within the path are not affected by this setting.
This might be in conflict with other options that affect the file
mode, like fsGroup, and the result can be other mode bits set.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].downwardAPI.items">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].downwardAPI.items</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Items is a list of downward API volume file</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].downwardAPI.items[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].downwardAPI.items[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>DownwardAPIVolumeFile represents information to create the file containing the pod field</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].downwardAPI.items[*].fieldRef">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].downwardAPI.items[*].fieldRef</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Required: Selects a field of the pod: only annotations, labels, name, namespace and uid are supported.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].downwardAPI.items[*].fieldRef.apiVersion">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].downwardAPI.items[*].fieldRef.apiVersion</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Version of the schema the FieldPath is written in terms of, defaults to &ldquo;v1&rdquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].downwardAPI.items[*].fieldRef.fieldPath">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].downwardAPI.items[*].fieldRef.fieldPath</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Path of the field to select in the specified API version.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].downwardAPI.items[*].mode">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].downwardAPI.items[*].mode</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Optional: mode bits used to set permissions on this file, must be an octal value
between 0000 and 0777 or a decimal value between 0 and 511.
YAML accepts both octal and decimal values, JSON requires decimal values for mode bits.
If not specified, the volume defaultMode will be used.
This might be in conflict with other options that affect the file
mode, like fsGroup, and the result can be other mode bits set.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].downwardAPI.items[*].path">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].downwardAPI.items[*].path</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Required: Path is  the relative path name of the file to be created. Must not be absolute or contain the &lsquo;..&rsquo; path. Must be utf-8 encoded. The first item of the relative path must not start with &lsquo;..&rsquo;</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].downwardAPI.items[*].resourceFieldRef">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].downwardAPI.items[*].resourceFieldRef</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Selects a resource of the container: only resources limits and requests
(limits.cpu, limits.memory, requests.cpu and requests.memory) are currently supported.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].downwardAPI.items[*].resourceFieldRef.containerName">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].downwardAPI.items[*].resourceFieldRef.containerName</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Container name: required for volumes, optional for env vars</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].downwardAPI.items[*].resourceFieldRef.divisor">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].downwardAPI.items[*].resourceFieldRef.divisor</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">


</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Specifies the output format of the exposed resources, defaults to &ldquo;1&rdquo;</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].downwardAPI.items[*].resourceFieldRef.resource">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].downwardAPI.items[*].resourceFieldRef.resource</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Required: resource to select</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].emptyDir">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].emptyDir</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>emptyDir represents a temporary directory that shares a pod&rsquo;s lifetime.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#emptydir">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#emptydir</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].emptyDir.medium">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].emptyDir.medium</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>medium represents what type of storage medium should back this directory.
The default is &ldquo;&rdquo; which means to use the node&rsquo;s default medium.
Must be an empty string (default) or Memory.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#emptydir">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#emptydir</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].emptyDir.sizeLimit">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].emptyDir.sizeLimit</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">


</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>sizeLimit is the total amount of local storage required for this EmptyDir volume.
The size limit is also applicable for memory medium.
The maximum usage on memory medium EmptyDir would be the minimum value between
the SizeLimit specified here and the sum of memory limits of all containers in a pod.
The default is nil which means that the limit is undefined.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#emptydir">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#emptydir</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>ephemeral represents a volume that is handled by a cluster storage driver.
The volume&rsquo;s lifecycle is tied to the pod that defines it - it will be created before the pod starts,
and deleted when the pod is removed.</p>

<p>Use this if:
a) the volume is only needed while the pod runs,
b) features of normal volumes like restoring from snapshot or capacity
   tracking are needed,
c) the storage driver is specified through a storage class, and
d) the storage driver supports dynamic volume provisioning through
   a PersistentVolumeClaim (see EphemeralVolumeSource for more
   information on the connection between this volume type
   and PersistentVolumeClaim).</p>

<p>Use PersistentVolumeClaim or one of the vendor-specific
APIs for volumes that persist for longer than the lifecycle
of an individual pod.</p>

<p>Use CSI for light-weight local ephemeral volumes if the CSI driver is meant to
be used that way - see the documentation of the driver for
more information.</p>

<p>A pod can use both types of ephemeral volumes and
persistent volumes at the same time.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Will be used to create a stand-alone PVC to provision the volume.
The pod in which this EphemeralVolumeSource is embedded will be the
owner of the PVC, i.e. the PVC will be deleted together with the
pod.  The name of the PVC will be <code>&lt;pod name&gt;-&lt;volume name&gt;</code> where
<code>&lt;volume name&gt;</code> is the name from the <code>PodSpec.Volumes</code> array
entry. Pod validation will reject the pod if the concatenated name
is not valid for a PVC (for example, too long).</p>

<p>An existing PVC with that name that is not owned by the pod
will <em>not</em> be used for the pod to avoid using an unrelated
volume by mistake. Starting the pod is then blocked until
the unrelated PVC is removed. If such a pre-created PVC is
meant to be used by the pod, the PVC has to updated with an
owner reference to the pod once the pod exists. Normally
this should not be necessary, but it may be useful when
manually reconstructing a broken cluster.</p>

<p>This field is read-only and no changes will be made by Kubernetes
to the PVC after it has been created.</p>

<p>Required, must not be nil.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.metadata">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.metadata</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>May contain labels and annotations that will be copied into the PVC
when creating it. No other fields are allowed and will be rejected during
validation.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The specification for the PersistentVolumeClaim. The entire content is
copied unchanged into the PVC that gets created from this
template. The same fields as in a PersistentVolumeClaim
are also valid here.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.accessModes">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.accessModes</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>accessModes contains the desired access modes the volume should have.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#access-modes-1">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#access-modes-1</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.accessModes[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.accessModes[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSource">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSource</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>dataSource field can be used to specify either:
* An existing VolumeSnapshot object (snapshot.storage.k8s.io/VolumeSnapshot)
* An existing PVC (PersistentVolumeClaim)
If the provisioner or an external controller can support the specified data source,
it will create a new volume based on the contents of the specified data source.
When the AnyVolumeDataSource feature gate is enabled, dataSource contents will be copied to dataSourceRef,
and dataSourceRef contents will be copied to dataSource when dataSourceRef.namespace is not specified.
If the namespace is specified, then dataSourceRef will not be copied to dataSource.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSource.apiGroup">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSource.apiGroup</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>APIGroup is the group for the resource being referenced.
If APIGroup is not specified, the specified Kind must be in the core API group.
For any other third-party types, APIGroup is required.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSource.kind">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSource.kind</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Kind is the type of resource being referenced</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSource.name">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSource.name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name is the name of resource being referenced</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSourceRef">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSourceRef</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>dataSourceRef specifies the object from which to populate the volume with data, if a non-empty
volume is desired. This may be any object from a non-empty API group (non
core object) or a PersistentVolumeClaim object.
When this field is specified, volume binding will only succeed if the type of
the specified object matches some installed volume populator or dynamic
provisioner.
This field will replace the functionality of the dataSource field and as such
if both fields are non-empty, they must have the same value. For backwards
compatibility, when namespace isn&rsquo;t specified in dataSourceRef,
both fields (dataSource and dataSourceRef) will be set to the same
value automatically if one of them is empty and the other is non-empty.
When namespace is specified in dataSourceRef,
dataSource isn&rsquo;t set to the same value and must be empty.
There are three important differences between dataSource and dataSourceRef:
* While dataSource only allows two specific types of objects, dataSourceRef
  allows any non-core object, as well as PersistentVolumeClaim objects.
* While dataSource ignores disallowed values (dropping them), dataSourceRef
  preserves all values, and generates an error if a disallowed value is
  specified.
* While dataSource only allows local objects, dataSourceRef allows objects
  in any namespaces.
(Beta) Using this field requires the AnyVolumeDataSource feature gate to be enabled.
(Alpha) Using the namespace field of dataSourceRef requires the CrossNamespaceVolumeDataSource feature gate to be enabled.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSourceRef.apiGroup">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSourceRef.apiGroup</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>APIGroup is the group for the resource being referenced.
If APIGroup is not specified, the specified Kind must be in the core API group.
For any other third-party types, APIGroup is required.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSourceRef.kind">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSourceRef.kind</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Kind is the type of resource being referenced</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSourceRef.name">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSourceRef.name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name is the name of resource being referenced</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSourceRef.namespace">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSourceRef.namespace</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Namespace is the namespace of resource being referenced
Note that when a namespace is specified, a gateway.networking.k8s.io/ReferenceGrant object is required in the referent namespace to allow that namespace&rsquo;s owner to accept the reference. See the ReferenceGrant documentation for details.
(Alpha) This field requires the CrossNamespaceVolumeDataSource feature gate to be enabled.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.resources">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.resources</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>resources represents the minimum resources the volume should have.
Users are allowed to specify resource requirements
that are lower than previous value but must still be higher than capacity recorded in the
status field of the claim.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#resources">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#resources</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.resources.limits">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.resources.limits</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Limits describes the maximum amount of compute resources allowed.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.resources.requests">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.resources.requests</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Requests describes the minimum amount of compute resources required.
If Requests is omitted for a container, it defaults to Limits if that is explicitly specified,
otherwise to an implementation-defined value. Requests cannot exceed Limits.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>selector is a label query over volumes to consider for binding.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector.matchExpressions">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector.matchExpressions</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>matchExpressions is a list of label selector requirements. The requirements are ANDed.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-11">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector.matchExpressions[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector.matchExpressions[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>A label selector requirement is a selector that contains values, a key, and an operator that
relates the key and values.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-12">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector.matchExpressions[*].key">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector.matchExpressions[*].key</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>key is the label key that the selector applies to.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-12">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector.matchExpressions[*].operator">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector.matchExpressions[*].operator</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>operator represents a key&rsquo;s relationship to a set of values.
Valid operators are In, NotIn, Exists and DoesNotExist.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-12">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector.matchExpressions[*].values">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector.matchExpressions[*].values</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>values is an array of string values. If the operator is In or NotIn,
the values array must be non-empty. If the operator is Exists or DoesNotExist,
the values array must be empty. This array is replaced during a strategic
merge patch.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-13">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector.matchExpressions[*].values[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector.matchExpressions[*].values[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector.matchLabels">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector.matchLabels</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>matchLabels is a map of {key,value} pairs. A single {key,value} in the matchLabels
map is equivalent to an element of matchExpressions, whose key field is &ldquo;key&rdquo;, the
operator is &ldquo;In&rdquo;, and the values array contains only &ldquo;value&rdquo;. The requirements are ANDed.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.storageClassName">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.storageClassName</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>storageClassName is the name of the StorageClass required by the claim.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#class-1">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#class-1</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.volumeAttributesClassName">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.volumeAttributesClassName</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>volumeAttributesClassName may be used to set the VolumeAttributesClass used by this claim.
If specified, the CSI driver will create or update the volume with the attributes defined
in the corresponding VolumeAttributesClass. This has a different purpose than storageClassName,
it can be changed after the claim is created. An empty string or nil value indicates that no
VolumeAttributesClass will be applied to the claim. If the claim enters an Infeasible error state,
this field can be reset to its previous value (including nil) to cancel the modification.
If the resource referred to by volumeAttributesClass does not exist, this PersistentVolumeClaim will be
set to a Pending state, as reflected by the modifyVolumeStatus field, until such as a resource
exists.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volume-attributes-classes/">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volume-attributes-classes/</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.volumeMode">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.volumeMode</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>volumeMode defines what type of volume is required by the claim.
Value of Filesystem is implied when not included in claim spec.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.volumeName">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.volumeName</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>volumeName is the binding reference to the PersistentVolume backing this claim.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].fc">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].fc</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>fc represents a Fibre Channel resource that is attached to a kubelet&rsquo;s host machine and then exposed to the pod.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].fc.fsType">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].fc.fsType</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>fsType is the filesystem type to mount.
Must be a filesystem type supported by the host operating system.
Ex. &ldquo;ext4&rdquo;, &ldquo;xfs&rdquo;, &ldquo;ntfs&rdquo;. Implicitly inferred to be &ldquo;ext4&rdquo; if unspecified.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].fc.lun">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].fc.lun</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>lun is Optional: FC target lun number</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].fc.readOnly">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].fc.readOnly</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>readOnly is Optional: Defaults to false (read/write). ReadOnly here will force
the ReadOnly setting in VolumeMounts.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].fc.targetWWNs">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].fc.targetWWNs</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>targetWWNs is Optional: FC target worldwide names (WWNs)</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].fc.targetWWNs[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].fc.targetWWNs[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].fc.wwids">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].fc.wwids</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>wwids Optional: FC volume world wide identifiers (wwids)
Either wwids or combination of targetWWNs and lun must be set, but not both simultaneously.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].fc.wwids[*]">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].fc.wwids[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].flexVolume">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].flexVolume</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>flexVolume represents a generic volume resource that is
provisioned/attached using an exec based plugin.
Deprecated: FlexVolume is deprecated. Consider using a CSIDriver instead.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].flexVolume.driver">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].flexVolume.driver</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>driver is the name of the driver to use for this volume.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].flexVolume.fsType">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].flexVolume.fsType</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>fsType is the filesystem type to mount.
Must be a filesystem type supported by the host operating system.
Ex. &ldquo;ext4&rdquo;, &ldquo;xfs&rdquo;, &ldquo;ntfs&rdquo;. The default filesystem depends on FlexVolume script.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].flexVolume.options">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].flexVolume.options</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>options is Optional: this field holds extra command options if any.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].flexVolume.readOnly">.spec.brokers[*].brokerConfig.volumes[*].flexVolume.readOnly</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>readOnly is Optional: defaults to false (read/write). ReadOnly here will force
the ReadOnly setting in VolumeMounts.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
