<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaceSelector.matchExpressions[*].values">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaceSelector.matchExpressions[*].values</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>values is an array of string values. If the operator is In or NotIn,
the values array must be non-empty. If the operator is Exists or DoesNotExist,
the values array must be empty. This array is replaced during a strategic
merge patch.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaceSelector.matchExpressions[*].values[*]">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaceSelector.matchExpressions[*].values[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaceSelector.matchLabels">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaceSelector.matchLabels</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>matchLabels is a map of {key,value} pairs. A single {key,value} in the matchLabels
map is equivalent to an element of matchExpressions, whose key field is &ldquo;key&rdquo;, the
operator is &ldquo;In&rdquo;, and the values array contains only &ldquo;value&rdquo;. The requirements are ANDed.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaces">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaces</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>namespaces specifies a static list of namespace names that the term applies to.
The term is applied to the union of the namespaces listed in this field
and the ones selected by namespaceSelector.
null or empty namespaces list and null namespaceSelector means &ldquo;this pod&rsquo;s namespace&rdquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaces[*]">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].namespaces[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].topologyKey">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].topologyKey</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>This pod should be co-located (affinity) or not co-located (anti-affinity) with the pods matching
the labelSelector in the specified namespaces, where co-located is defined as running on a node
whose value of the label with key topologyKey matches that of any node on which any of the
selected pods is running.
Empty topologyKey is not allowed.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-2">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.capacityConfig">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.capacityConfig</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Annotations to be applied to CruiseControl pod</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-2">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.cruiseControlEndpoint">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.cruiseControlEndpoint</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-2">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.cruiseControlOperationSpec">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.cruiseControlOperationSpec</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>CruiseControlOperationSpec specifies the configuration of the CruiseControlOperation handling</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-3">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.cruiseControlOperationSpec.ttlSecondsAfterFinished">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.cruiseControlOperationSpec.ttlSecondsAfterFinished</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>When TTLSecondsAfterFinished is specified, the created and finished (completed successfully or completedWithError and errorPolicy: ignore)
cruiseControlOperation custom resource will be deleted after the given time elapsed.
When it is 0 then the resource is going to be deleted instantly after the operation is finished.
When it is not specified the resource is not going to be removed.
Value can be only zero and positive integers.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-2">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.cruiseControlTaskSpec">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.cruiseControlTaskSpec</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-3">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.cruiseControlTaskSpec.RetryDurationMinutes">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.cruiseControlTaskSpec.RetryDurationMinutes</h3>

<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>RetryDurationMinutes describes the amount of time the Operator waits for the task</p>

This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names</a></p>

<div class="property depth-2">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>InitContainers add extra initContainers to CruiseControl pod</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-3">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*]">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>A single application container that you want to run within a pod.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].args">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].args</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Arguments to the entrypoint.
The container image&rsquo;s CMD is used if this is not provided.
Variable references $(VAR_NAME) are expanded using the container&rsquo;s environment. If a variable
cannot be resolved, the reference in the input string will be unchanged. Double $$ are reduced
to a single $, which allows for escaping the $(VAR_NAME) syntax: i.e. &ldquo;$$(VAR_NAME)&rdquo; will
produce the string literal &ldquo;$(VAR_NAME)&rdquo;. Escaped references will never be expanded, regardless
of whether the variable exists or not. Cannot be updated.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-command-argument-container/#running-a-command-in-a-shell">https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-command-argument-container/#running-a-command-in-a-shell</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].args[*]">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].args[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].command">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].command</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Entrypoint array. Not executed within a shell.
The container image&rsquo;s ENTRYPOINT is used if this is not provided.
Variable references $(VAR_NAME) are expanded using the container&rsquo;s environment. If a variable
cannot be resolved, the reference in the input string will be unchanged. Double $$ are reduced
to a single $, which allows for escaping the $(VAR_NAME) syntax: i.e. &ldquo;$$(VAR_NAME)&rdquo; will
produce the string literal &ldquo;$(VAR_NAME)&rdquo;. Escaped references will never be expanded, regardless
of whether the variable exists or not. Cannot be updated.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-command-argument-container/#running-a-command-in-a-shell">https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-command-argument-container/#running-a-command-in-a-shell</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].command[*]">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].command[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>List of environment variables to set in the container.
Cannot be updated.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].envFrom">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].envFrom</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>List of sources to populate environment variables in the container.
The keys defined within a source may consist of any printable ASCII characters except &lsquo;=&rsquo;.
When a key exists in multiple
sources, the value associated with the last source will take precedence.
Values defined by an Env with a duplicate key will take precedence.
Cannot be updated.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].envFrom[*]">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].envFrom[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>EnvFromSource represents the source of a set of ConfigMaps or Secrets</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].envFrom[*].configMapRef">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].envFrom[*].configMapRef</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The ConfigMap to select from</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].envFrom[*].configMapRef.name">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].envFrom[*].configMapRef.name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].envFrom[*].configMapRef.optional">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].envFrom[*].configMapRef.optional</h3>
</div>

<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">

</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Optional text to prepend to the name of each environment variable.
May consist of any printable ASCII characters except &lsquo;=&rsquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">

</div>

</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].envFrom[*].secretRef.optional">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].envFrom[*].secretRef.optional</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*]">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>EnvVar represents an environment variable present in a Container.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].name">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name of the environment variable.
May consist of any printable ASCII characters except &lsquo;=&rsquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].value">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].value</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Variable references $(VAR_NAME) are expanded
using the previously defined environment variables in the container and
any service environment variables. If a variable cannot be resolved,
the reference in the input string will be unchanged. Double $$ are reduced
to a single $, which allows for escaping the $(VAR_NAME) syntax: i.e.
&ldquo;$$(VAR_NAME)&rdquo; will produce the string literal &ldquo;$(VAR_NAME)&rdquo;.
Escaped references will never be expanded, regardless of whether the variable
exists or not.
Defaults to &ldquo;&rdquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom</h3>

</div>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.configMapKeyRef">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.configMapKeyRef</h3>

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">

<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.configMapKeyRef.optional">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.configMapKeyRef.optional</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Specify whether the ConfigMap or its key must be defined</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fieldRef">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fieldRef</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Selects a field of the pod: supports metadata.name, metadata.namespace, <code>metadata.labels['&lt;KEY&gt;']</code>, <code>metadata.annotations['&lt;KEY&gt;']</code>,
spec.nodeName, spec.serviceAccountName, status.hostIP, status.podIP, status.podIPs.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fieldRef.apiVersion">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fieldRef.apiVersion</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Version of the schema the FieldPath is written in terms of, defaults to &ldquo;v1&rdquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fieldRef.fieldPath">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fieldRef.fieldPath</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Path of the field to select in the specified API version.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fileKeyRef">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fileKeyRef</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>FileKeyRef selects a key of the env file.
Requires the EnvFiles feature gate to be enabled.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fileKeyRef.key">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fileKeyRef.key</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The key within the env file. An invalid key will prevent the pod from starting.
The keys defined within a source may consist of any printable ASCII characters except &lsquo;=&rsquo;.
During Alpha stage of the EnvFiles feature gate, the key size is limited to 128 characters.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fileKeyRef.optional">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fileKeyRef.optional</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Specify whether the file or its key must be defined. If the file or key
does not exist, then the env var is not published.
If optional is set to true and the specified key does not exist,
the environment variable will not be set in the Pod&rsquo;s containers.</p>

<p>If optional is set to false and the specified key does not exist,
an error will be returned during Pod creation.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fileKeyRef.path">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fileKeyRef.path</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The path within the volume from which to select the file.
Must be relative and may not contain the &lsquo;..&rsquo; path or start with &lsquo;..&rsquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fileKeyRef.volumeName">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.fileKeyRef.volumeName</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The name of the volume mount containing the env file.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.resourceFieldRef">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.resourceFieldRef</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Selects a resource of the container: only resources limits and requests
(limits.cpu, limits.memory, limits.ephemeral-storage, requests.cpu, requests.memory and requests.ephemeral-storage) are currently supported.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.resourceFieldRef.containerName">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.resourceFieldRef.containerName</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Container name: required for volumes, optional for env vars</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.resourceFieldRef.divisor">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.resourceFieldRef.divisor</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">


</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Specifies the output format of the exposed resources, defaults to &ldquo;1&rdquo;</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.resourceFieldRef.resource">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.resourceFieldRef.resource</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Required: resource to select</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.secretKeyRef">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.secretKeyRef</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Selects a key of a secret in the pod&rsquo;s namespace</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.secretKeyRef.key">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.secretKeyRef.key</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The key of the secret to select from.  Must be a valid secret key.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.secretKeyRef.name">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.secretKeyRef.name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.secretKeyRef.optional">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].env[*].valueFrom.secretKeyRef.optional</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Specify whether the Secret or its key must be defined</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].image">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].image</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Container image name.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images</a>
This field is optional to allow higher level config management to default or override
container images in workload controllers like Deployments and StatefulSets.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].imagePullPolicy">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].imagePullPolicy</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Image pull policy.
One of Always, Never, IfNotPresent.
Defaults to Always if :latest tag is specified, or IfNotPresent otherwise.
Cannot be updated.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images#updating-images">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images#updating-images</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Actions that the management system should take in response to container lifecycle events.
Cannot be updated.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>PostStart is called immediately after a container is created. If the handler fails,
the container is terminated and restarted according to its restart policy.
Other management of the container blocks until the hook completes.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/container-lifecycle-hooks/#container-hooks">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/container-lifecycle-hooks/#container-hooks</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.exec">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.exec</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Exec specifies a command to execute in the container.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.exec.command">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.exec.command</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Command is the command line to execute inside the container, the working directory for the
command  is root (&lsquo;/&rsquo;) in the container&rsquo;s filesystem. The command is simply exec&rsquo;d, it is
not run inside a shell, so traditional shell instructions (&lsquo;|&rsquo;, etc) won&rsquo;t work. To use
a shell, you need to explicitly call out to that shell.
Exit status of 0 is treated as live/healthy and non-zero is unhealthy.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.exec.command[*]">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.exec.command[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>HTTPGet specifies an HTTP GET request to perform.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.host">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.host</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Host name to connect to, defaults to the pod IP. You probably want to set
&ldquo;Host&rdquo; in httpHeaders instead.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.httpHeaders">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.httpHeaders</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Custom headers to set in the request. HTTP allows repeated headers.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.httpHeaders[*]">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.httpHeaders[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>HTTPHeader describes a custom header to be used in HTTP probes</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.httpHeaders[*].name">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.httpHeaders[*].name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The header field name.
This will be canonicalized upon output, so case-variant names will be understood as the same header.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.httpHeaders[*].value">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.httpHeaders[*].value</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The header field value</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.path">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.path</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Path to access on the HTTP server.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.port">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.port</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">

<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name or number of the port to access on the container.
Number must be in the range 1 to 65535.
Name must be an IANA_SVC_NAME.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.scheme">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.httpGet.scheme</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Scheme to use for connecting to the host.
Defaults to HTTP.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.sleep">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.sleep</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Sleep represents a duration that the container should sleep.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.sleep.seconds">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.sleep.seconds</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Seconds is the number of seconds to sleep.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.tcpSocket">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.tcpSocket</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Deprecated. TCPSocket is NOT supported as a LifecycleHandler and kept
for backward compatibility. There is no validation of this field and
lifecycle hooks will fail at runtime when it is specified.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.tcpSocket.host">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.tcpSocket.host</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Optional: Host name to connect to, defaults to the pod IP.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.tcpSocket.port">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.postStart.tcpSocket.port</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">

<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Number or name of the port to access on the container.
Number must be in the range 1 to 65535.
Name must be an IANA_SVC_NAME.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.preStop">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.preStop</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>PreStop is called immediately before a container is terminated due to an
API request or management event such as liveness/startup probe failure,
preemption, resource contention, etc. The handler is not called if the
container crashes or exits. The Pod&rsquo;s termination grace period countdown begins before the
PreStop hook is executed. Regardless of the outcome of the handler, the
container will eventually terminate within the Pod&rsquo;s termination grace
period (unless delayed by finalizers). Other management of the container blocks until the hook completes
or until the termination grace period is reached.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/container-lifecycle-hooks/#container-hooks">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/container-lifecycle-hooks/#container-hooks</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.preStop.exec">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.preStop.exec</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Exec specifies a command to execute in the container.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.preStop.exec.command">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.preStop.exec.command</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Command is the command line to execute inside the container, the working directory for the
command  is root (&lsquo;/&rsquo;) in the container&rsquo;s filesystem. The command is simply exec&rsquo;d, it is
not run inside a shell, so traditional shell instructions (&lsquo;|&rsquo;, etc) won&rsquo;t work. To use
a shell, you need to explicitly call out to that shell.
Exit status of 0 is treated as live/healthy and non-zero is unhealthy.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

for backward compatibility. There is no validation of this field and

</div>

</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.preStop.tcpSocket.port">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.preStop.tcpSocket.port</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">

<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

Number must be in the range 1 to 65535.
Name must be an IANA_SVC_NAME.</p>

</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.stopSignal">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].lifecycle.stopSignal</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>StopSignal defines which signal will be sent to a container when it is being stopped.
If not specified, the default is defined by the container runtime in use.
StopSignal can only be set for Pods with a non-empty .spec.os.name</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Periodic probe of container liveness.
Container will be restarted if the probe fails.
Cannot be updated.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.exec">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.exec</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Exec specifies a command to execute in the container.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.exec.command">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.exec.command</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Command is the command line to execute inside the container, the working directory for the
command  is root (&lsquo;/&rsquo;) in the container&rsquo;s filesystem. The command is simply exec&rsquo;d, it is
not run inside a shell, so traditional shell instructions (&lsquo;|&rsquo;, etc) won&rsquo;t work. To use
a shell, you need to explicitly call out to that shell.
Exit status of 0 is treated as live/healthy and non-zero is unhealthy.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.exec.command[*]">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.exec.command[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.failureThreshold">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.failureThreshold</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Minimum consecutive failures for the probe to be considered failed after having succeeded.
Defaults to 3. Minimum value is 1.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.grpc">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.grpc</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>GRPC specifies a GRPC HealthCheckRequest.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.grpc.port">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.grpc.port</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Port number of the gRPC service. Number must be in the range 1 to 65535.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.grpc.service">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.grpc.service</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Service is the name of the service to place in the gRPC HealthCheckRequest
(see <a href="https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/doc/health-checking.md">https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/doc/health-checking.md</a>).</p>

<p>If this is not specified, the default behavior is defined by gRPC.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.httpGet">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.httpGet</h3>

</div>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property-header">

</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<p>Host name to connect to, defaults to the pod IP. You probably want to set
&ldquo;Host&rdquo; in httpHeaders instead.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property-header">

</div>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders[*]">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders[*]</h3>

<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders[*].name">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders[*].name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The header field name.
This will be canonicalized upon output, so case-variant names will be understood as the same header.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders[*].value">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders[*].value</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The header field value</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.httpGet.path">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.httpGet.path</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Path to access on the HTTP server.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.httpGet.port">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.httpGet.port</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">

<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name or number of the port to access on the container.
Number must be in the range 1 to 65535.
Name must be an IANA_SVC_NAME.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.httpGet.scheme">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.httpGet.scheme</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Scheme to use for connecting to the host.
Defaults to HTTP.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.initialDelaySeconds">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.initialDelaySeconds</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Number of seconds after the container has started before liveness probes are initiated.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.periodSeconds">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.periodSeconds</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>How often (in seconds) to perform the probe.
Default to 10 seconds. Minimum value is 1.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.successThreshold">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.successThreshold</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Minimum consecutive successes for the probe to be considered successful after having failed.
Defaults to 1. Must be 1 for liveness and startup. Minimum value is 1.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.tcpSocket">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.tcpSocket</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>TCPSocket specifies a connection to a TCP port.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.tcpSocket.host">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.tcpSocket.host</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Optional: Host name to connect to, defaults to the pod IP.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.tcpSocket.port">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.tcpSocket.port</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">

<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Number or name of the port to access on the container.
Number must be in the range 1 to 65535.
Name must be an IANA_SVC_NAME.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Optional duration in seconds the pod needs to terminate gracefully upon probe failure.
The grace period is the duration in seconds after the processes running in the pod are sent
a termination signal and the time when the processes are forcibly halted with a kill signal.
Set this value longer than the expected cleanup time for your process.
If this value is nil, the pod&rsquo;s terminationGracePeriodSeconds will be used. Otherwise, this
value overrides the value provided by the pod spec.
Value must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via
the kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).
This is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.
Minimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.timeoutSeconds">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].livenessProbe.timeoutSeconds</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Number of seconds after which the probe times out.
Defaults to 1 second. Minimum value is 1.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].name">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name of the container specified as a DNS_LABEL.
Each container in a pod must have a unique name (DNS_LABEL).
Cannot be updated.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].ports">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].ports</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>List of ports to expose from the container. Not specifying a port here
DOES NOT prevent that port from being exposed. Any port which is
listening on the default &ldquo;0.0.0.0&rdquo; address inside a container will be
accessible from the network.
Modifying this array with strategic merge patch may corrupt the data.
For more information See <a href="https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/108255">https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/108255</a>.
Cannot be updated.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].ports[*]">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].ports[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>ContainerPort represents a network port in a single container.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].ports[*].containerPort">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].ports[*].containerPort</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Number of port to expose on the pod&rsquo;s IP address.
This must be a valid port number, 0 &lt; x &lt; 65536.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].ports[*].hostIP">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].ports[*].hostIP</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>What host IP to bind the external port to.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].ports[*].hostPort">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].ports[*].hostPort</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Number of port to expose on the host.
If specified, this must be a valid port number, 0 &lt; x &lt; 65536.
If HostNetwork is specified, this must match ContainerPort.
Most containers do not need this.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].ports[*].name">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].ports[*].name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>If specified, this must be an IANA_SVC_NAME and unique within the pod. Each
named port in a pod must have a unique name. Name for the port that can be
referred to by services.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].ports[*].protocol">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].ports[*].protocol</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Protocol for port. Must be UDP, TCP, or SCTP.
Defaults to &ldquo;TCP&rdquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Periodic probe of container service readiness.
Container will be removed from service endpoints if the probe fails.
Cannot be updated.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.exec">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.exec</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Exec specifies a command to execute in the container.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.exec.command">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.exec.command</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Command is the command line to execute inside the container, the working directory for the
command  is root (&lsquo;/&rsquo;) in the container&rsquo;s filesystem. The command is simply exec&rsquo;d, it is
not run inside a shell, so traditional shell instructions (&lsquo;|&rsquo;, etc) won&rsquo;t work. To use
a shell, you need to explicitly call out to that shell.
Exit status of 0 is treated as live/healthy and non-zero is unhealthy.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.exec.command[*]">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.exec.command[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.failureThreshold">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.failureThreshold</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Minimum consecutive failures for the probe to be considered failed after having succeeded.
Defaults to 3. Minimum value is 1.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.grpc">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.grpc</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>GRPC specifies a GRPC HealthCheckRequest.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.grpc.port">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.grpc.port</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Port number of the gRPC service. Number must be in the range 1 to 65535.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.grpc.service">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.grpc.service</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Service is the name of the service to place in the gRPC HealthCheckRequest
(see <a href="https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/doc/health-checking.md">https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/doc/health-checking.md</a>).</p>

<p>If this is not specified, the default behavior is defined by gRPC.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>HTTPGet specifies an HTTP GET request to perform.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.host">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.host</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Host name to connect to, defaults to the pod IP. You probably want to set
&ldquo;Host&rdquo; in httpHeaders instead.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Custom headers to set in the request. HTTP allows repeated headers.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders[*]">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>HTTPHeader describes a custom header to be used in HTTP probes</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders[*].name">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders[*].name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The header field name.
This will be canonicalized upon output, so case-variant names will be understood as the same header.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders[*].value">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.httpHeaders[*].value</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The header field value</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.path">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.path</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Path to access on the HTTP server.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.port">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.port</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">

<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name or number of the port to access on the container.
Number must be in the range 1 to 65535.
Name must be an IANA_SVC_NAME.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.scheme">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.httpGet.scheme</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Scheme to use for connecting to the host.
Defaults to HTTP.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.initialDelaySeconds">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.initialDelaySeconds</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Number of seconds after the container has started before liveness probes are initiated.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.periodSeconds">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.periodSeconds</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>How often (in seconds) to perform the probe.
Default to 10 seconds. Minimum value is 1.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.successThreshold">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.successThreshold</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Minimum consecutive successes for the probe to be considered successful after having failed.
Defaults to 1. Must be 1 for liveness and startup. Minimum value is 1.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.tcpSocket">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.tcpSocket</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>TCPSocket specifies a connection to a TCP port.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.tcpSocket.host">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.tcpSocket.host</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Optional: Host name to connect to, defaults to the pod IP.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.tcpSocket.port">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.tcpSocket.port</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">

<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Number or name of the port to access on the container.
Number must be in the range 1 to 65535.
Name must be an IANA_SVC_NAME.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Optional duration in seconds the pod needs to terminate gracefully upon probe failure.
The grace period is the duration in seconds after the processes running in the pod are sent
a termination signal and the time when the processes are forcibly halted with a kill signal.
Set this value longer than the expected cleanup time for your process.
If this value is nil, the pod&rsquo;s terminationGracePeriodSeconds will be used. Otherwise, this
value overrides the value provided by the pod spec.
Value must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via
the kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).
This is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.
Minimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.timeoutSeconds">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].readinessProbe.timeoutSeconds</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Number of seconds after which the probe times out.
Defaults to 1 second. Minimum value is 1.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].resizePolicy">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].resizePolicy</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Resources resize policy for the container.
This field cannot be set on ephemeral containers.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].resizePolicy[*]">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].resizePolicy[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>ContainerResizePolicy represents resource resize policy for the container.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].resizePolicy[*].resourceName">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.initContainers[*].resizePolicy[*].resourceName</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name of the resource to which this resource resize policy applies.
Supported values: cpu, memory.</p>

</div>
