<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSource.name">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSource.name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name is the name of resource being referenced</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSourceRef">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSourceRef</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>dataSourceRef specifies the object from which to populate the volume with data, if a non-empty
volume is desired. This may be any object from a non-empty API group (non
core object) or a PersistentVolumeClaim object.
When this field is specified, volume binding will only succeed if the type of
the specified object matches some installed volume populator or dynamic
provisioner.
This field will replace the functionality of the dataSource field and as such
if both fields are non-empty, they must have the same value. For backwards
compatibility, when namespace isn&rsquo;t specified in dataSourceRef,
both fields (dataSource and dataSourceRef) will be set to the same
value automatically if one of them is empty and the other is non-empty.
When namespace is specified in dataSourceRef,
dataSource isn&rsquo;t set to the same value and must be empty.
There are three important differences between dataSource and dataSourceRef:
* While dataSource only allows two specific types of objects, dataSourceRef
  allows any non-core object, as well as PersistentVolumeClaim objects.
* While dataSource ignores disallowed values (dropping them), dataSourceRef
  preserves all values, and generates an error if a disallowed value is
  specified.
* While dataSource only allows local objects, dataSourceRef allows objects
  in any namespaces.
(Beta) Using this field requires the AnyVolumeDataSource feature gate to be enabled.
(Alpha) Using the namespace field of dataSourceRef requires the CrossNamespaceVolumeDataSource feature gate to be enabled.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSourceRef.apiGroup">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSourceRef.apiGroup</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>APIGroup is the group for the resource being referenced.
If APIGroup is not specified, the specified Kind must be in the core API group.
For any other third-party types, APIGroup is required.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSourceRef.kind">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSourceRef.kind</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Kind is the type of resource being referenced</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSourceRef.name">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSourceRef.name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name is the name of resource being referenced</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSourceRef.namespace">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSourceRef.namespace</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Namespace is the namespace of resource being referenced
Note that when a namespace is specified, a gateway.networking.k8s.io/ReferenceGrant object is required in the referent namespace to allow that namespace&rsquo;s owner to accept the reference. See the ReferenceGrant documentation for details.
(Alpha) This field requires the CrossNamespaceVolumeDataSource feature gate to be enabled.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.resources">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.resources</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>resources represents the minimum resources the volume should have.
Users are allowed to specify resource requirements
that are lower than previous value but must still be higher than capacity recorded in the
status field of the claim.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#resources">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#resources</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.resources.limits">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.resources.limits</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Limits describes the maximum amount of compute resources allowed.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.resources.requests">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.resources.requests</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Requests describes the minimum amount of compute resources required.
If Requests is omitted for a container, it defaults to Limits if that is explicitly specified,
otherwise to an implementation-defined value. Requests cannot exceed Limits.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>selector is a label query over volumes to consider for binding.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector.matchExpressions">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector.matchExpressions</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>matchExpressions is a list of label selector requirements. The requirements are ANDed.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector.matchExpressions[*]">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector.matchExpressions[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>A label selector requirement is a selector that contains values, a key, and an operator that
relates the key and values.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector.matchExpressions[*].key">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector.matchExpressions[*].key</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>key is the label key that the selector applies to.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector.matchExpressions[*].operator">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector.matchExpressions[*].operator</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>operator represents a key&rsquo;s relationship to a set of values.
Valid operators are In, NotIn, Exists and DoesNotExist.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector.matchExpressions[*].values">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector.matchExpressions[*].values</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>values is an array of string values. If the operator is In or NotIn,
the values array must be non-empty. If the operator is Exists or DoesNotExist,
the values array must be empty. This array is replaced during a strategic
merge patch.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-11">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector.matchExpressions[*].values[*]">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector.matchExpressions[*].values[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector.matchLabels">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.selector.matchLabels</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>matchLabels is a map of {key,value} pairs. A single {key,value} in the matchLabels
map is equivalent to an element of matchExpressions, whose key field is &ldquo;key&rdquo;, the
operator is &ldquo;In&rdquo;, and the values array contains only &ldquo;value&rdquo;. The requirements are ANDed.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.storageClassName">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.storageClassName</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>storageClassName is the name of the StorageClass required by the claim.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#class-1">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#class-1</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.volumeAttributesClassName">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.volumeAttributesClassName</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>volumeAttributesClassName may be used to set the VolumeAttributesClass used by this claim.
If specified, the CSI driver will create or update the volume with the attributes defined
in the corresponding VolumeAttributesClass. This has a different purpose than storageClassName,
it can be changed after the claim is created. An empty string or nil value indicates that no
VolumeAttributesClass will be applied to the claim. If the claim enters an Infeasible error state,
this field can be reset to its previous value (including nil) to cancel the modification.
If the resource referred to by volumeAttributesClass does not exist, this PersistentVolumeClaim will be
set to a Pending state, as reflected by the modifyVolumeStatus field, until such as a resource
exists.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volume-attributes-classes/">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volume-attributes-classes/</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.volumeMode">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.volumeMode</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>volumeMode defines what type of volume is required by the claim.
Value of Filesystem is implied when not included in claim spec.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.volumeName">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.volumeName</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>volumeName is the binding reference to the PersistentVolume backing this claim.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].fc">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].fc</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>fc represents a Fibre Channel resource that is attached to a kubelet&rsquo;s host machine and then exposed to the pod.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].fc.fsType">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].fc.fsType</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>fsType is the filesystem type to mount.
Must be a filesystem type supported by the host operating system.
Ex. &ldquo;ext4&rdquo;, &ldquo;xfs&rdquo;, &ldquo;ntfs&rdquo;. Implicitly inferred to be &ldquo;ext4&rdquo; if unspecified.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].fc.lun">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].fc.lun</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>lun is Optional: FC target lun number</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].fc.readOnly">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].fc.readOnly</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>readOnly is Optional: Defaults to false (read/write). ReadOnly here will force
the ReadOnly setting in VolumeMounts.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].fc.targetWWNs">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].fc.targetWWNs</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>targetWWNs is Optional: FC target worldwide names (WWNs)</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].fc.targetWWNs[*]">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].fc.targetWWNs[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].fc.wwids">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].fc.wwids</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>wwids Optional: FC volume world wide identifiers (wwids)
Either wwids or combination of targetWWNs and lun must be set, but not both simultaneously.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].fc.wwids[*]">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].fc.wwids[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].flexVolume">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].flexVolume</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>flexVolume represents a generic volume resource that is
provisioned/attached using an exec based plugin.
Deprecated: FlexVolume is deprecated. Consider using a CSIDriver instead.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].flexVolume.driver">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].flexVolume.driver</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>driver is the name of the driver to use for this volume.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].flexVolume.fsType">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].flexVolume.fsType</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>fsType is the filesystem type to mount.
Must be a filesystem type supported by the host operating system.
Ex. &ldquo;ext4&rdquo;, &ldquo;xfs&rdquo;, &ldquo;ntfs&rdquo;. The default filesystem depends on FlexVolume script.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].flexVolume.options">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].flexVolume.options</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>options is Optional: this field holds extra command options if any.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].flexVolume.readOnly">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].flexVolume.readOnly</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>readOnly is Optional: defaults to false (read/write). ReadOnly here will force
the ReadOnly setting in VolumeMounts.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].flexVolume.secretRef">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].flexVolume.secretRef</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>secretRef is Optional: secretRef is reference to the secret object containing
sensitive information to pass to the plugin scripts. This may be
empty if no secret object is specified. If the secret object
contains more than one secret, all secrets are passed to the plugin
scripts.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].flexVolume.secretRef.name">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].flexVolume.secretRef.name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].flocker">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].flocker</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>flocker represents a Flocker volume attached to a kubelet&rsquo;s host machine. This depends on the Flocker control service being running.
Deprecated: Flocker is deprecated and the in-tree flocker type is no longer supported.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].flocker.datasetName">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].flocker.datasetName</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>datasetName is Name of the dataset stored as metadata -&gt; name on the dataset for Flocker
should be considered as deprecated</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].flocker.datasetUUID">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].flocker.datasetUUID</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>datasetUUID is the UUID of the dataset. This is unique identifier of a Flocker dataset</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].gcePersistentDisk">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].gcePersistentDisk</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>gcePersistentDisk represents a GCE Disk resource that is attached to a
kubelet&rsquo;s host machine and then exposed to the pod.
Deprecated: GCEPersistentDisk is deprecated. All operations for the in-tree
gcePersistentDisk type are redirected to the pd.csi.storage.gke.io CSI driver.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#gcepersistentdisk">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#gcepersistentdisk</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].gcePersistentDisk.fsType">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].gcePersistentDisk.fsType</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>fsType is filesystem type of the volume that you want to mount.
Tip: Ensure that the filesystem type is supported by the host operating system.
Examples: &ldquo;ext4&rdquo;, &ldquo;xfs&rdquo;, &ldquo;ntfs&rdquo;. Implicitly inferred to be &ldquo;ext4&rdquo; if unspecified.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#gcepersistentdisk">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#gcepersistentdisk</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].gcePersistentDisk.partition">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].gcePersistentDisk.partition</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>partition is the partition in the volume that you want to mount.
If omitted, the default is to mount by volume name.
Examples: For volume /dev/sda1, you specify the partition as &ldquo;1&rdquo;.
Similarly, the volume partition for /dev/sda is &ldquo;0&rdquo; (or you can leave the property empty).
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#gcepersistentdisk">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#gcepersistentdisk</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].gcePersistentDisk.pdName">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].gcePersistentDisk.pdName</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>pdName is unique name of the PD resource in GCE. Used to identify the disk in GCE.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#gcepersistentdisk">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#gcepersistentdisk</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].gcePersistentDisk.readOnly">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].gcePersistentDisk.readOnly</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>readOnly here will force the ReadOnly setting in VolumeMounts.
Defaults to false.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#gcepersistentdisk">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#gcepersistentdisk</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].gitRepo">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].gitRepo</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>gitRepo represents a git repository at a particular revision.
Deprecated: GitRepo is deprecated. To provision a container with a git repo, mount an
EmptyDir into an InitContainer that clones the repo using git, then mount the EmptyDir
into the Pod&rsquo;s container.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].gitRepo.directory">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].gitRepo.directory</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>directory is the target directory name.
Must not contain or start with &lsquo;..&rsquo;.  If &lsquo;.&rsquo; is supplied, the volume directory will be the
git repository.  Otherwise, if specified, the volume will contain the git repository in
the subdirectory with the given name.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].gitRepo.repository">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].gitRepo.repository</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>repository is the URL</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].gitRepo.revision">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].gitRepo.revision</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>revision is the commit hash for the specified revision.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].glusterfs">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].glusterfs</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>glusterfs represents a Glusterfs mount on the host that shares a pod&rsquo;s lifetime.
Deprecated: Glusterfs is deprecated and the in-tree glusterfs type is no longer supported.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].glusterfs.endpoints">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].glusterfs.endpoints</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>endpoints is the endpoint name that details Glusterfs topology.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].glusterfs.path">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].glusterfs.path</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>path is the Glusterfs volume path.
More info: <a href="https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/glusterfs/README.md#create-a-pod">https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/glusterfs/README.md#create-a-pod</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].glusterfs.readOnly">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].glusterfs.readOnly</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>readOnly here will force the Glusterfs volume to be mounted with read-only permissions.
Defaults to false.
More info: <a href="https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/glusterfs/README.md#create-a-pod">https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/glusterfs/README.md#create-a-pod</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].hostPath">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].hostPath</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>hostPath represents a pre-existing file or directory on the host
machine that is directly exposed to the container. This is generally
used for system agents or other privileged things that are allowed
to see the host machine. Most containers will NOT need this.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#hostpath">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#hostpath</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].hostPath.path">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].hostPath.path</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>path of the directory on the host.
If the path is a symlink, it will follow the link to the real path.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#hostpath">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#hostpath</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].hostPath.type">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].hostPath.type</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>type for HostPath Volume
Defaults to &ldquo;&rdquo;
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#hostpath">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#hostpath</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].image">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].image</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>image represents an OCI object (a container image or artifact) pulled and mounted on the kubelet&rsquo;s host machine.
The volume is resolved at pod startup depending on which PullPolicy value is provided:</p>

<ul>
<li>Always: the kubelet always attempts to pull the reference. Container creation will fail If the pull fails.</li>
<li>Never: the kubelet never pulls the reference and only uses a local image or artifact. Container creation will fail if the reference isn&rsquo;t present.</li>
<li>IfNotPresent: the kubelet pulls if the reference isn&rsquo;t already present on disk. Container creation will fail if the reference isn&rsquo;t present and the pull fails.</li>
</ul>

<p>The volume gets re-resolved if the pod gets deleted and recreated, which means that new remote content will become available on pod recreation.
A failure to resolve or pull the image during pod startup will block containers from starting and may add significant latency. Failures will be retried using normal volume backoff and will be reported on the pod reason and message.
The types of objects that may be mounted by this volume are defined by the container runtime implementation on a host machine and at minimum must include all valid types supported by the container image field.
The OCI object gets mounted in a single directory (spec.containers[<em>].volumeMounts.mountPath) by merging the manifest layers in the same way as for container images.
The volume will be mounted read-only (ro).
Sub path mounts for containers are not supported (spec.containers[</em>].volumeMounts.subpath) before 1.33.
The field spec.securityContext.fsGroupChangePolicy has no effect on this volume type.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].image.pullPolicy">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].image.pullPolicy</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Policy for pulling OCI objects. Possible values are:
Always: the kubelet always attempts to pull the reference. Container creation will fail If the pull fails.
Never: the kubelet never pulls the reference and only uses a local image or artifact. Container creation will fail if the reference isn&rsquo;t present.
IfNotPresent: the kubelet pulls if the reference isn&rsquo;t already present on disk. Container creation will fail if the reference isn&rsquo;t present and the pull fails.
Defaults to Always if :latest tag is specified, or IfNotPresent otherwise.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].image.reference">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].image.reference</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Required: Image or artifact reference to be used.
Behaves in the same way as pod.spec.containers[*].image.
Pull secrets will be assembled in the same way as for the container image by looking up node credentials, SA image pull secrets, and pod spec image pull secrets.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images</a>
This field is optional to allow higher level config management to default or override
container images in workload controllers like Deployments and StatefulSets.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].iscsi">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].iscsi</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>iscsi represents an ISCSI Disk resource that is attached to a
kubelet&rsquo;s host machine and then exposed to the pod.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes/#iscsi">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes/#iscsi</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].iscsi.chapAuthDiscovery">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].iscsi.chapAuthDiscovery</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>chapAuthDiscovery defines whether support iSCSI Discovery CHAP authentication</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].iscsi.chapAuthSession">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].iscsi.chapAuthSession</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>chapAuthSession defines whether support iSCSI Session CHAP authentication</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].iscsi.fsType">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].iscsi.fsType</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>fsType is the filesystem type of the volume that you want to mount.
Tip: Ensure that the filesystem type is supported by the host operating system.
Examples: &ldquo;ext4&rdquo;, &ldquo;xfs&rdquo;, &ldquo;ntfs&rdquo;. Implicitly inferred to be &ldquo;ext4&rdquo; if unspecified.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#iscsi">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#iscsi</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].iscsi.initiatorName">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].iscsi.initiatorName</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>initiatorName is the custom iSCSI Initiator Name.
If initiatorName is specified with iscsiInterface simultaneously, new iSCSI interface
<target portal>:<volume name> will be created for the connection.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].iscsi.iqn">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].iscsi.iqn</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>iqn is the target iSCSI Qualified Name.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].iscsi.iscsiInterface">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].iscsi.iscsiInterface</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>iscsiInterface is the interface Name that uses an iSCSI transport.
Defaults to &lsquo;default&rsquo; (tcp).</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].iscsi.lun">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].iscsi.lun</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>lun represents iSCSI Target Lun number.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].iscsi.portals">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].iscsi.portals</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>portals is the iSCSI Target Portal List. The portal is either an IP or ip_addr:port if the port
is other than default (typically TCP ports 860 and 3260).</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].iscsi.portals[*]">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].iscsi.portals[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].iscsi.readOnly">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].iscsi.readOnly</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>readOnly here will force the ReadOnly setting in VolumeMounts.
Defaults to false.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].iscsi.secretRef">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].iscsi.secretRef</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>secretRef is the CHAP Secret for iSCSI target and initiator authentication</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].iscsi.secretRef.name">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].iscsi.secretRef.name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].iscsi.targetPortal">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].iscsi.targetPortal</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>targetPortal is iSCSI Target Portal. The Portal is either an IP or ip_addr:port if the port
is other than default (typically TCP ports 860 and 3260).</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].name">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>name of the volume.
Must be a DNS_LABEL and unique within the pod.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].nfs">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].nfs</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>nfs represents an NFS mount on the host that shares a pod&rsquo;s lifetime
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#nfs">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#nfs</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].nfs.path">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].nfs.path</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>path that is exported by the NFS server.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#nfs">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#nfs</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].nfs.readOnly">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].nfs.readOnly</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>readOnly here will force the NFS export to be mounted with read-only permissions.
Defaults to false.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#nfs">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#nfs</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].nfs.server">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].nfs.server</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>server is the hostname or IP address of the NFS server.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#nfs">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#nfs</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].persistentVolumeClaim">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].persistentVolumeClaim</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>persistentVolumeClaimVolumeSource represents a reference to a
PersistentVolumeClaim in the same namespace.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#persistentvolumeclaims">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#persistentvolumeclaims</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].persistentVolumeClaim.claimName">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].persistentVolumeClaim.claimName</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>claimName is the name of a PersistentVolumeClaim in the same namespace as the pod using this volume.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#persistentvolumeclaims">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#persistentvolumeclaims</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].persistentVolumeClaim.readOnly">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].persistentVolumeClaim.readOnly</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>readOnly Will force the ReadOnly setting in VolumeMounts.
Default false.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].photonPersistentDisk">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].photonPersistentDisk</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>photonPersistentDisk represents a PhotonController persistent disk attached and mounted on kubelets host machine.
Deprecated: PhotonPersistentDisk is deprecated and the in-tree photonPersistentDisk type is no longer supported.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].photonPersistentDisk.fsType">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].photonPersistentDisk.fsType</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>fsType is the filesystem type to mount.
Must be a filesystem type supported by the host operating system.
Ex. &ldquo;ext4&rdquo;, &ldquo;xfs&rdquo;, &ldquo;ntfs&rdquo;. Implicitly inferred to be &ldquo;ext4&rdquo; if unspecified.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].photonPersistentDisk.pdID">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].photonPersistentDisk.pdID</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>pdID is the ID that identifies Photon Controller persistent disk</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].portworxVolume">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].portworxVolume</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>portworxVolume represents a portworx volume attached and mounted on kubelets host machine.
Deprecated: PortworxVolume is deprecated. All operations for the in-tree portworxVolume type
are redirected to the pxd.portworx.com CSI driver.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].portworxVolume.fsType">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].portworxVolume.fsType</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>fSType represents the filesystem type to mount
Must be a filesystem type supported by the host operating system.
Ex. &ldquo;ext4&rdquo;, &ldquo;xfs&rdquo;. Implicitly inferred to be &ldquo;ext4&rdquo; if unspecified.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].portworxVolume.readOnly">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].portworxVolume.readOnly</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>readOnly defaults to false (read/write). ReadOnly here will force
the ReadOnly setting in VolumeMounts.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].portworxVolume.volumeID">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].portworxVolume.volumeID</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>volumeID uniquely identifies a Portworx volume</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>projected items for all in one resources secrets, configmaps, and downward API</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.defaultMode">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.defaultMode</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>defaultMode are the mode bits used to set permissions on created files by default.
Must be an octal value between 0000 and 0777 or a decimal value between 0 and 511.
YAML accepts both octal and decimal values, JSON requires decimal values for mode bits.
Directories within the path are not affected by this setting.
This might be in conflict with other options that affect the file
mode, like fsGroup, and the result can be other mode bits set.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>sources is the list of volume projections. Each entry in this list
handles one source.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*]">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Projection that may be projected along with other supported volume types.
Exactly one of these fields must be set.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].clusterTrustBundle">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].clusterTrustBundle</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>ClusterTrustBundle allows a pod to access the <code>.spec.trustBundle</code> field
of ClusterTrustBundle objects in an auto-updating file.</p>

<p>Alpha, gated by the ClusterTrustBundleProjection feature gate.</p>

<p>ClusterTrustBundle objects can either be selected by name, or by the
combination of signer name and a label selector.</p>

<p>Kubelet performs aggressive normalization of the PEM contents written
into the pod filesystem.  Esoteric PEM features such as inter-block
comments and block headers are stripped.  Certificates are deduplicated.
The ordering of certificates within the file is arbitrary, and Kubelet
may change the order over time.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].clusterTrustBundle.labelSelector">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].clusterTrustBundle.labelSelector</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Select all ClusterTrustBundles that match this label selector.  Only has
effect if signerName is set.  Mutually-exclusive with name.  If unset,
interpreted as &ldquo;match nothing&rdquo;.  If set but empty, interpreted as &ldquo;match
everything&rdquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].clusterTrustBundle.labelSelector.matchExpressions">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].clusterTrustBundle.labelSelector.matchExpressions</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>matchExpressions is a list of label selector requirements. The requirements are ANDed.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].clusterTrustBundle.labelSelector.matchExpressions[*]">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].clusterTrustBundle.labelSelector.matchExpressions[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>A label selector requirement is a selector that contains values, a key, and an operator that
relates the key and values.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-11">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].clusterTrustBundle.labelSelector.matchExpressions[*].key">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].clusterTrustBundle.labelSelector.matchExpressions[*].key</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>key is the label key that the selector applies to.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-11">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].clusterTrustBundle.labelSelector.matchExpressions[*].operator">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].clusterTrustBundle.labelSelector.matchExpressions[*].operator</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>operator represents a key&rsquo;s relationship to a set of values.
Valid operators are In, NotIn, Exists and DoesNotExist.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-11">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].clusterTrustBundle.labelSelector.matchExpressions[*].values">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].clusterTrustBundle.labelSelector.matchExpressions[*].values</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>values is an array of string values. If the operator is In or NotIn,
the values array must be non-empty. If the operator is Exists or DoesNotExist,
the values array must be empty. This array is replaced during a strategic
merge patch.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-12">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].clusterTrustBundle.labelSelector.matchExpressions[*].values[*]">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].clusterTrustBundle.labelSelector.matchExpressions[*].values[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].clusterTrustBundle.labelSelector.matchLabels">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].clusterTrustBundle.labelSelector.matchLabels</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>matchLabels is a map of {key,value} pairs. A single {key,value} in the matchLabels
map is equivalent to an element of matchExpressions, whose key field is &ldquo;key&rdquo;, the
operator is &ldquo;In&rdquo;, and the values array contains only &ldquo;value&rdquo;. The requirements are ANDed.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].clusterTrustBundle.name">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].clusterTrustBundle.name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Select a single ClusterTrustBundle by object name.  Mutually-exclusive
with signerName and labelSelector.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].clusterTrustBundle.optional">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].clusterTrustBundle.optional</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>If true, don&rsquo;t block pod startup if the referenced ClusterTrustBundle(s)
aren&rsquo;t available.  If using name, then the named ClusterTrustBundle is
allowed not to exist.  If using signerName, then the combination of
signerName and labelSelector is allowed to match zero
ClusterTrustBundles.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].clusterTrustBundle.path">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].clusterTrustBundle.path</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Relative path from the volume root to write the bundle.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].clusterTrustBundle.signerName">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].clusterTrustBundle.signerName</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Select all ClusterTrustBundles that match this signer name.
Mutually-exclusive with name.  The contents of all selected
ClusterTrustBundles will be unified and deduplicated.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].configMap">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].configMap</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>configMap information about the configMap data to project</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].configMap.items">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].configMap.items</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>items if unspecified, each key-value pair in the Data field of the referenced
ConfigMap will be projected into the volume as a file whose name is the
key and content is the value. If specified, the listed keys will be
projected into the specified paths, and unlisted keys will not be
present. If a key is specified which is not present in the ConfigMap,
the volume setup will error unless it is marked optional. Paths must be
relative and may not contain the &lsquo;..&rsquo; path or start with &lsquo;..&rsquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].configMap.items[*]">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].configMap.items[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Maps a string key to a path within a volume.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].configMap.items[*].key">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].configMap.items[*].key</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>key is the key to project.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].configMap.items[*].mode">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].configMap.items[*].mode</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>mode is Optional: mode bits used to set permissions on this file.
Must be an octal value between 0000 and 0777 or a decimal value between 0 and 511.
YAML accepts both octal and decimal values, JSON requires decimal values for mode bits.
If not specified, the volume defaultMode will be used.
This might be in conflict with other options that affect the file
mode, like fsGroup, and the result can be other mode bits set.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].configMap.items[*].path">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].configMap.items[*].path</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>path is the relative path of the file to map the key to.
May not be an absolute path.
May not contain the path element &lsquo;..&rsquo;.
May not start with the string &lsquo;..&rsquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].configMap.name">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].configMap.name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].configMap.optional">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].configMap.optional</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>optional specify whether the ConfigMap or its keys must be defined</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].downwardAPI">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].downwardAPI</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>downwardAPI information about the downwardAPI data to project</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].downwardAPI.items">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].downwardAPI.items</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Items is a list of DownwardAPIVolume file</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].downwardAPI.items[*]">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].downwardAPI.items[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>DownwardAPIVolumeFile represents information to create the file containing the pod field</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].downwardAPI.items[*].fieldRef">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].downwardAPI.items[*].fieldRef</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Required: Selects a field of the pod: only annotations, labels, name, namespace and uid are supported.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-11">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].downwardAPI.items[*].fieldRef.apiVersion">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].downwardAPI.items[*].fieldRef.apiVersion</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Version of the schema the FieldPath is written in terms of, defaults to &ldquo;v1&rdquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-11">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].downwardAPI.items[*].fieldRef.fieldPath">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].downwardAPI.items[*].fieldRef.fieldPath</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Path of the field to select in the specified API version.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].downwardAPI.items[*].mode">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].downwardAPI.items[*].mode</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Optional: mode bits used to set permissions on this file, must be an octal value
between 0000 and 0777 or a decimal value between 0 and 511.
YAML accepts both octal and decimal values, JSON requires decimal values for mode bits.
If not specified, the volume defaultMode will be used.
This might be in conflict with other options that affect the file
mode, like fsGroup, and the result can be other mode bits set.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].downwardAPI.items[*].path">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].downwardAPI.items[*].path</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Required: Path is  the relative path name of the file to be created. Must not be absolute or contain the &lsquo;..&rsquo; path. Must be utf-8 encoded. The first item of the relative path must not start with &lsquo;..&rsquo;</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].downwardAPI.items[*].resourceFieldRef">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].downwardAPI.items[*].resourceFieldRef</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Selects a resource of the container: only resources limits and requests
(limits.cpu, limits.memory, requests.cpu and requests.memory) are currently supported.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-11">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].downwardAPI.items[*].resourceFieldRef.containerName">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].downwardAPI.items[*].resourceFieldRef.containerName</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Container name: required for volumes, optional for env vars</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-11">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].downwardAPI.items[*].resourceFieldRef.divisor">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].downwardAPI.items[*].resourceFieldRef.divisor</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">


</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Specifies the output format of the exposed resources, defaults to &ldquo;1&rdquo;</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-11">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].downwardAPI.items[*].resourceFieldRef.resource">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].downwardAPI.items[*].resourceFieldRef.resource</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Required: resource to select</p>

</div>

</div>
