</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].podCertificate">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].podCertificate</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Projects an auto-rotating credential bundle (private key and certificate
chain) that the pod can use either as a TLS client or server.</p>

<p>Kubelet generates a private key and uses it to send a
PodCertificateRequest to the named signer.  Once the signer approves the
request and issues a certificate chain, Kubelet writes the key and
certificate chain to the pod filesystem.  The pod does not start until
certificates have been issued for each podCertificate projected volume
source in its spec.</p>

<p>Kubelet will begin trying to rotate the certificate at the time indicated
by the signer using the PodCertificateRequest.Status.BeginRefreshAt
timestamp.</p>

<p>Kubelet can write a single file, indicated by the credentialBundlePath
field, or separate files, indicated by the keyPath and
certificateChainPath fields.</p>

<p>The credential bundle is a single file in PEM format.  The first PEM
entry is the private key (in PKCS#8 format), and the remaining PEM
entries are the certificate chain issued by the signer (typically,
signers will return their certificate chain in leaf-to-root order).</p>

<p>Prefer using the credential bundle format, since your application code
can read it atomically.  If you use keyPath and certificateChainPath,
your application must make two separate file reads. If these coincide
with a certificate rotation, it is possible that the private key and leaf
certificate you read may not correspond to each other.  Your application
will need to check for this condition, and re-read until they are
consistent.</p>

<p>The named signer controls chooses the format of the certificate it
issues; consult the signer implementation&rsquo;s documentation to learn how to
use the certificates it issues.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].podCertificate.certificateChainPath">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].podCertificate.certificateChainPath</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Write the certificate chain at this path in the projected volume.</p>

<p>Most applications should use credentialBundlePath.  When using keyPath
and certificateChainPath, your application needs to check that the key
and leaf certificate are consistent, because it is possible to read the
files mid-rotation.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].podCertificate.credentialBundlePath">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].podCertificate.credentialBundlePath</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Write the credential bundle at this path in the projected volume.</p>

<p>The credential bundle is a single file that contains multiple PEM blocks.
The first PEM block is a PRIVATE KEY block, containing a PKCS#8 private
key.</p>

<p>The remaining blocks are CERTIFICATE blocks, containing the issued
certificate chain from the signer (leaf and any intermediates).</p>

<p>Using credentialBundlePath lets your Pod&rsquo;s application code make a single
atomic read that retrieves a consistent key and certificate chain.  If you
project them to separate files, your application code will need to
additionally check that the leaf certificate was issued to the key.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].podCertificate.keyPath">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].podCertificate.keyPath</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Write the key at this path in the projected volume.</p>

<p>Most applications should use credentialBundlePath.  When using keyPath
and certificateChainPath, your application needs to check that the key
and leaf certificate are consistent, because it is possible to read the
files mid-rotation.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].podCertificate.keyType">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].podCertificate.keyType</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The type of keypair Kubelet will generate for the pod.</p>

<p>Valid values are &ldquo;RSA3072&rdquo;, &ldquo;RSA4096&rdquo;, &ldquo;ECDSAP256&rdquo;, &ldquo;ECDSAP384&rdquo;,
&ldquo;ECDSAP521&rdquo;, and &ldquo;ED25519&rdquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].podCertificate.maxExpirationSeconds">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].podCertificate.maxExpirationSeconds</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>maxExpirationSeconds is the maximum lifetime permitted for the
certificate.</p>

<p>Kubelet copies this value verbatim into the PodCertificateRequests it
generates for this projection.</p>

<p>If omitted, kube-apiserver will set it to 86400(24 hours). kube-apiserver
will reject values shorter than 3600 (1 hour).  The maximum allowable
value is 7862400 (91 days).</p>

<p>The signer implementation is then free to issue a certificate with any
lifetime <em>shorter</em> than MaxExpirationSeconds, but no shorter than 3600
seconds (1 hour).  This constraint is enforced by kube-apiserver.
<code>kubernetes.io</code> signers will never issue certificates with a lifetime
longer than 24 hours.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].podCertificate.signerName">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].podCertificate.signerName</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Kubelet&rsquo;s generated CSRs will be addressed to this signer.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].podCertificate.userAnnotations">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].podCertificate.userAnnotations</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>userAnnotations allow pod authors to pass additional information to
the signer implementation.  Kubernetes does not restrict or validate this
metadata in any way.</p>

<p>These values are copied verbatim into the <code>spec.unverifiedUserAnnotations</code> field of
the PodCertificateRequest objects that Kubelet creates.</p>

<p>Entries are subject to the same validation as object metadata annotations,
with the addition that all keys must be domain-prefixed. No restrictions
are placed on values, except an overall size limitation on the entire field.</p>

<p>Signers should document the keys and values they support. Signers should
deny requests that contain keys they do not recognize.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].secret">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].secret</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>secret information about the secret data to project</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].secret.items">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].secret.items</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>items if unspecified, each key-value pair in the Data field of the referenced
Secret will be projected into the volume as a file whose name is the
key and content is the value. If specified, the listed keys will be
projected into the specified paths, and unlisted keys will not be
present. If a key is specified which is not present in the Secret,
the volume setup will error unless it is marked optional. Paths must be
relative and may not contain the &lsquo;..&rsquo; path or start with &lsquo;..&rsquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].secret.items[*]">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].secret.items[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Maps a string key to a path within a volume.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].secret.items[*].key">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].secret.items[*].key</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>key is the key to project.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].secret.items[*].mode">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].secret.items[*].mode</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>mode is Optional: mode bits used to set permissions on this file.
Must be an octal value between 0000 and 0777 or a decimal value between 0 and 511.
YAML accepts both octal and decimal values, JSON requires decimal values for mode bits.
If not specified, the volume defaultMode will be used.
This might be in conflict with other options that affect the file
mode, like fsGroup, and the result can be other mode bits set.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].secret.items[*].path">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].secret.items[*].path</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>path is the relative path of the file to map the key to.
May not be an absolute path.
May not contain the path element &lsquo;..&rsquo;.
May not start with the string &lsquo;..&rsquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].secret.name">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].secret.name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].secret.optional">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].secret.optional</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>optional field specify whether the Secret or its key must be defined</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].serviceAccountToken">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].serviceAccountToken</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>serviceAccountToken is information about the serviceAccountToken data to project</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].serviceAccountToken.audience">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].serviceAccountToken.audience</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>audience is the intended audience of the token. A recipient of a token
must identify itself with an identifier specified in the audience of the
token, and otherwise should reject the token. The audience defaults to the
identifier of the apiserver.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].serviceAccountToken.expirationSeconds">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].serviceAccountToken.expirationSeconds</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>expirationSeconds is the requested duration of validity of the service
account token. As the token approaches expiration, the kubelet volume
plugin will proactively rotate the service account token. The kubelet will
start trying to rotate the token if the token is older than 80 percent of
its time to live or if the token is older than 24 hours.Defaults to 1 hour
and must be at least 10 minutes.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].serviceAccountToken.path">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].projected.sources[*].serviceAccountToken.path</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>path is the path relative to the mount point of the file to project the
token into.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].quobyte">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].quobyte</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>quobyte represents a Quobyte mount on the host that shares a pod&rsquo;s lifetime.
Deprecated: Quobyte is deprecated and the in-tree quobyte type is no longer supported.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].quobyte.group">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].quobyte.group</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>group to map volume access to
Default is no group</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].quobyte.readOnly">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].quobyte.readOnly</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>readOnly here will force the Quobyte volume to be mounted with read-only permissions.
Defaults to false.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].quobyte.registry">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].quobyte.registry</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>registry represents a single or multiple Quobyte Registry services
specified as a string as host:port pair (multiple entries are separated with commas)
which acts as the central registry for volumes</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].quobyte.tenant">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].quobyte.tenant</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>tenant owning the given Quobyte volume in the Backend
Used with dynamically provisioned Quobyte volumes, value is set by the plugin</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].quobyte.user">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].quobyte.user</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>user to map volume access to
Defaults to serivceaccount user</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].quobyte.volume">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].quobyte.volume</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>volume is a string that references an already created Quobyte volume by name.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].rbd">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].rbd</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>rbd represents a Rados Block Device mount on the host that shares a pod&rsquo;s lifetime.
Deprecated: RBD is deprecated and the in-tree rbd type is no longer supported.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].rbd.fsType">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].rbd.fsType</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>fsType is the filesystem type of the volume that you want to mount.
Tip: Ensure that the filesystem type is supported by the host operating system.
Examples: &ldquo;ext4&rdquo;, &ldquo;xfs&rdquo;, &ldquo;ntfs&rdquo;. Implicitly inferred to be &ldquo;ext4&rdquo; if unspecified.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#rbd">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#rbd</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].rbd.image">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].rbd.image</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>image is the rados image name.
More info: <a href="https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md#how-to-use-it">https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md#how-to-use-it</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].rbd.keyring">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].rbd.keyring</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>keyring is the path to key ring for RBDUser.
Default is /etc/ceph/keyring.
More info: <a href="https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md#how-to-use-it">https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md#how-to-use-it</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].rbd.monitors">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].rbd.monitors</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>monitors is a collection of Ceph monitors.
More info: <a href="https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md#how-to-use-it">https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md#how-to-use-it</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].rbd.monitors[*]">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].rbd.monitors[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].rbd.pool">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].rbd.pool</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>pool is the rados pool name.
Default is rbd.
More info: <a href="https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md#how-to-use-it">https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md#how-to-use-it</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].rbd.readOnly">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].rbd.readOnly</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>readOnly here will force the ReadOnly setting in VolumeMounts.
Defaults to false.
More info: <a href="https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md#how-to-use-it">https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md#how-to-use-it</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].rbd.secretRef">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].rbd.secretRef</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>secretRef is name of the authentication secret for RBDUser. If provided
overrides keyring.
Default is nil.
More info: <a href="https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md#how-to-use-it">https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md#how-to-use-it</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].rbd.secretRef.name">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].rbd.secretRef.name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].rbd.user">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].rbd.user</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>user is the rados user name.
Default is admin.
More info: <a href="https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md#how-to-use-it">https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md#how-to-use-it</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].scaleIO">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].scaleIO</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>scaleIO represents a ScaleIO persistent volume attached and mounted on Kubernetes nodes.
Deprecated: ScaleIO is deprecated and the in-tree scaleIO type is no longer supported.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].scaleIO.fsType">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].scaleIO.fsType</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>fsType is the filesystem type to mount.
Must be a filesystem type supported by the host operating system.
Ex. &ldquo;ext4&rdquo;, &ldquo;xfs&rdquo;, &ldquo;ntfs&rdquo;.
Default is &ldquo;xfs&rdquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].scaleIO.gateway">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].scaleIO.gateway</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>gateway is the host address of the ScaleIO API Gateway.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].scaleIO.protectionDomain">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].scaleIO.protectionDomain</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>protectionDomain is the name of the ScaleIO Protection Domain for the configured storage.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].scaleIO.readOnly">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].scaleIO.readOnly</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>readOnly Defaults to false (read/write). ReadOnly here will force
the ReadOnly setting in VolumeMounts.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].scaleIO.secretRef">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].scaleIO.secretRef</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>secretRef references to the secret for ScaleIO user and other
sensitive information. If this is not provided, Login operation will fail.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].scaleIO.secretRef.name">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].scaleIO.secretRef.name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].scaleIO.sslEnabled">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].scaleIO.sslEnabled</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>sslEnabled Flag enable/disable SSL communication with Gateway, default false</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].scaleIO.storageMode">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].scaleIO.storageMode</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>storageMode indicates whether the storage for a volume should be ThickProvisioned or ThinProvisioned.
Default is ThinProvisioned.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].scaleIO.storagePool">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].scaleIO.storagePool</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>storagePool is the ScaleIO Storage Pool associated with the protection domain.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].scaleIO.system">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].scaleIO.system</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>system is the name of the storage system as configured in ScaleIO.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].scaleIO.volumeName">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].scaleIO.volumeName</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>volumeName is the name of a volume already created in the ScaleIO system
that is associated with this volume source.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].secret">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].secret</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>secret represents a secret that should populate this volume.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#secret">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#secret</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].secret.defaultMode">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].secret.defaultMode</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>defaultMode is Optional: mode bits used to set permissions on created files by default.
Must be an octal value between 0000 and 0777 or a decimal value between 0 and 511.
YAML accepts both octal and decimal values, JSON requires decimal values
for mode bits. Defaults to 0644.
Directories within the path are not affected by this setting.
This might be in conflict with other options that affect the file
mode, like fsGroup, and the result can be other mode bits set.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].secret.items">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].secret.items</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>items If unspecified, each key-value pair in the Data field of the referenced
Secret will be projected into the volume as a file whose name is the
key and content is the value. If specified, the listed keys will be
projected into the specified paths, and unlisted keys will not be
present. If a key is specified which is not present in the Secret,
the volume setup will error unless it is marked optional. Paths must be
relative and may not contain the &lsquo;..&rsquo; path or start with &lsquo;..&rsquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].secret.items[*]">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].secret.items[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Maps a string key to a path within a volume.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].secret.items[*].key">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].secret.items[*].key</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>key is the key to project.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].secret.items[*].mode">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].secret.items[*].mode</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>mode is Optional: mode bits used to set permissions on this file.
Must be an octal value between 0000 and 0777 or a decimal value between 0 and 511.
YAML accepts both octal and decimal values, JSON requires decimal values for mode bits.
If not specified, the volume defaultMode will be used.
This might be in conflict with other options that affect the file
mode, like fsGroup, and the result can be other mode bits set.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].secret.items[*].path">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].secret.items[*].path</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>path is the relative path of the file to map the key to.
May not be an absolute path.
May not contain the path element &lsquo;..&rsquo;.
May not start with the string &lsquo;..&rsquo;.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].secret.optional">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].secret.optional</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>optional field specify whether the Secret or its keys must be defined</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].secret.secretName">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].secret.secretName</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>secretName is the name of the secret in the pod&rsquo;s namespace to use.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#secret">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#secret</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].storageos">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].storageos</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>storageOS represents a StorageOS volume attached and mounted on Kubernetes nodes.
Deprecated: StorageOS is deprecated and the in-tree storageos type is no longer supported.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].storageos.fsType">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].storageos.fsType</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>fsType is the filesystem type to mount.
Must be a filesystem type supported by the host operating system.
Ex. &ldquo;ext4&rdquo;, &ldquo;xfs&rdquo;, &ldquo;ntfs&rdquo;. Implicitly inferred to be &ldquo;ext4&rdquo; if unspecified.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].storageos.readOnly">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].storageos.readOnly</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>readOnly defaults to false (read/write). ReadOnly here will force
the ReadOnly setting in VolumeMounts.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].storageos.secretRef">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].storageos.secretRef</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>secretRef specifies the secret to use for obtaining the StorageOS API
credentials.  If not specified, default values will be attempted.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].storageos.secretRef.name">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].storageos.secretRef.name</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names">https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names</a></p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].storageos.volumeName">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].storageos.volumeName</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>volumeName is the human-readable name of the StorageOS volume.  Volume
names are only unique within a namespace.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].storageos.volumeNamespace">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].storageos.volumeNamespace</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>volumeNamespace specifies the scope of the volume within StorageOS.  If no
namespace is specified then the Pod&rsquo;s namespace will be used.  This allows the
Kubernetes name scoping to be mirrored within StorageOS for tighter integration.
Set VolumeName to any name to override the default behaviour.
Set to &ldquo;default&rdquo; if you are not using namespaces within StorageOS.
Namespaces that do not pre-exist within StorageOS will be created.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].vsphereVolume">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].vsphereVolume</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>vsphereVolume represents a vSphere volume attached and mounted on kubelets host machine.
Deprecated: VsphereVolume is deprecated. All operations for the in-tree vsphereVolume type
are redirected to the csi.vsphere.vmware.com CSI driver.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].vsphereVolume.fsType">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].vsphereVolume.fsType</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>fsType is filesystem type to mount.
Must be a filesystem type supported by the host operating system.
Ex. &ldquo;ext4&rdquo;, &ldquo;xfs&rdquo;, &ldquo;ntfs&rdquo;. Implicitly inferred to be &ldquo;ext4&rdquo; if unspecified.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].vsphereVolume.storagePolicyID">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].vsphereVolume.storagePolicyID</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>storagePolicyID is the storage Policy Based Management (SPBM) profile ID associated with the StoragePolicyName.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].vsphereVolume.storagePolicyName">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].vsphereVolume.storagePolicyName</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>storagePolicyName is the storage Policy Based Management (SPBM) profile name.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].vsphereVolume.volumePath">.spec.cruiseControlConfig.volumes[*].vsphereVolume.volumePath</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>volumePath is the path that identifies vSphere volume vmdk</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-1">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.disruptionBudget">.spec.disruptionBudget</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>DisruptionBudget defines the configuration for PodDisruptionBudget where the workload is managed by the kafka-operator</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-2">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.disruptionBudget.budget">.spec.disruptionBudget.budget</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The budget to set for the PDB, can either be static number or a percentage</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-2">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.disruptionBudget.create">.spec.disruptionBudget.create</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">boolean</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>If set to true, will create a podDisruptionBudget</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-1">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.envoyConfig">.spec.envoyConfig</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>EnvoyConfig defines the config for Envoy</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-2">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.envoyConfig.adminPort">.spec.envoyConfig.adminPort</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Envoy admin port</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-2">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.envoyConfig.affinity">.spec.envoyConfig.affinity</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Affinity is a group of affinity scheduling rules.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-3">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity">.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Describes node affinity scheduling rules for the pod.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution">.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy
the affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose
a node that violates one or more of the expressions. The node that is
most preferred is the one with the greatest sum of weights, i.e.
for each node that meets all of the scheduling requirements (resource
request, requiredDuringScheduling affinity expressions, etc.),
compute a sum by iterating through the elements of this field and adding
&ldquo;weight&rdquo; to the sum if the node matches the corresponding matchExpressions; the
node(s) with the highest sum are the most preferred.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*]">.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>An empty preferred scheduling term matches all objects with implicit weight 0
(i.e. it&rsquo;s a no-op). A null preferred scheduling term matches no objects (i.e. is also a no-op).</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].preference">.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].preference</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>A node selector term, associated with the corresponding weight.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].preference.matchExpressions">.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].preference.matchExpressions</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>A list of node selector requirements by node&rsquo;s labels.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-8">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].preference.matchExpressions[*]">.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].preference.matchExpressions[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>A node selector requirement is a selector that contains values, a key, and an operator
that relates the key and values.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].preference.matchExpressions[*].key">.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].preference.matchExpressions[*].key</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>The label key that the selector applies to.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].preference.matchExpressions[*].operator">.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].preference.matchExpressions[*].operator</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Represents a key&rsquo;s relationship to a set of values.
Valid operators are In, NotIn, Exists, DoesNotExist. Gt, and Lt.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-9">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].preference.matchExpressions[*].values">.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].preference.matchExpressions[*].values</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>An array of string values. If the operator is In or NotIn,
the values array must be non-empty. If the operator is Exists or DoesNotExist,
the values array must be empty. If the operator is Gt or Lt, the values
array must have a single element, which will be interpreted as an integer.
This array is replaced during a strategic merge patch.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property-header">

</div>

<span class="property-type">array</span>

</div>

the values array must be non-empty. If the operator is Exists or DoesNotExist,
the values array must be empty. If the operator is Gt or Lt, the values
array must have a single element, which will be interpreted as an integer.
This array is replaced during a strategic merge patch.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-10">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].preference.matchFields[*].values[*]">.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].preference.matchFields[*].values[*]</h3>
</div>

<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">string</span>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].weight">.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[*].weight</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">integer</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Weight associated with matching the corresponding nodeSelectorTerm, in the range 1-100.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-4">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution">.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>If the affinity requirements specified by this field are not met at
scheduling time, the pod will not be scheduled onto the node.
If the affinity requirements specified by this field cease to be met
at some point during pod execution (e.g. due to an update), the system
may or may not try to eventually evict the pod from its node.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-5">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution.nodeSelectorTerms">.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution.nodeSelectorTerms</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">array</span>
<span class="property-required">Required</span>
</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>Required. A list of node selector terms. The terms are ORed.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-6">
<div class="property-header">
<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution.nodeSelectorTerms[*]">.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution.nodeSelectorTerms[*]</h3>
</div>
<div class="property-body">
<div class="property-meta">
<span class="property-type">object</span>

</div>

<div class="property-description">
<p>A null or empty node selector term matches no objects. The requirements of
them are ANDed.
The TopologySelectorTerm type implements a subset of the NodeSelectorTerm.</p>

</div>

</div>
</div>

<div class="property depth-7">
<div class="property-header">

</div>
</div>

</div>

<h3 class="property-path" id="v1beta1-.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution.nodeSelectorTerms[*].matchExpressions[*].values[*]">.spec.envoyConfig.affinity.nodeAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution.nodeSelectorTerms[*].matchExpressions[*].values[*]</h3>
